issue_title: Tiny race condition in curio.local
issue_body:
Theoretically, someone might be calling `curio.run` simultaneously from two different threads, and theoretically, those two runs might both end up using some code from a single library, and theoretically, that library might contain a `curio.Local`. And if all that were true, then theoretically it might happen that the two threads both attempted to access some attribute of that `curio.Local` object at the same time.

If this were to happen, then it's possible that one thread would see the other thread's local values, because currently `curio.Local` mutates the object in-place (see `_patch_magic_dict`) and doesn't have any locking.

I doubt anyone has hit this in real life, but since I noticed it I figured I'd mention it :-)

issue_title: Add self referencing functionality to wraps
issue_body:
In order to support uses cases where the unit does not matter, but it has to be the same as some other argument. It would be convenient to have the ability to reference another argument from wraps.

 Idea for an API:
```
from pint import Any, SameAs
@ureg.wraps(Any('value'), SameAs('value'))
def func(value):
    return value + 1

>>> func(3 * ureg.meter)
4 * meter
>>> func(3 * ureg.second)
4 * meter
```
Notice that the body of `func` receives the value `3` (not the quantity), adds 1 and then put the same units. 'value' is just a name for the argument. Any unique name or number can be used. We do not use the name of func because this can be unknown.

Questions:
0. Other names for the API?
1. Avoid names when unambiguous.
2. Units raised to some power. Shall we allow them? With which API?
2.a.- `SameAs('value') ** 2`: intuitive
2.b.- `SameAs('value', 2)`: easier to implement

issue_title: Support for new PacBio BAM format?
issue_body:
Hi Heng,

Do you have any plans to support the new [PB unaligned BAM](http://pacbiofileformats.readthedocs.io/en/5.0/BAM.html) format as a MiniMap2 input? 

The old .h5 format is being phased out and all Sequel Subreads are now given as PB BAM. This new format does not contain quality scores for individual subreads (All Phred scores default to 0 when converted to FASTQ) but does contain tags with per base pulse information that is used by Arrow for genome polishing.

It would be great if MiniMap2 could map Subreads from PB BAM and add the pulse tag data back to the final SAM alignment.

Thanks,
Adam

issue_title: Handle setdefault on proxy dicts
issue_body:
With 2.0.0, the following pattern I was using is broken:
```python
command.environment.setdefault('variables', []).append(value)
```
The idea here is that `command['environment']['variables']` is a list of dicts, but any part of that path might or might not exist. In 1.x, the `command.environment` would actually insert a `environment` key into `command`. In 2.0.0, the `environment` dict is created but not inserted into the parent, because there is no `setitem`.

Proposal: all mutating member functions of `Dict` (specifically including `setdefault`) should insert into the parent.

issue_title: Bottleneck of update() - pandas operations
issue_body:
For simple strategies `bt.core.StrategyBase.update()` and `bt.core.SecurityBase.update()` are the bottlenecks in terms of performance. E.g. this is a profiling for a simple (SelectAll - WeightEqually - Rebalance) strategy for ~100 assets and 45 years of history:
```
Line #      Hits         Time  Per Hit   % Time  Line Contents
==============================================================
   138                                               @profile
   139                                               def run(self):
   140                                                   """
   141                                                   Runs the Backtest.
   142                                                   """
   143                                                   # set run flag
   144         1            4      4.0      0.0          self.has_run = True
   145                                           
   146                                                   # setup strategy
   147         1         1823   1823.0      0.0          self.strategy.setup(self.data)
   148                                           
   149                                                   # adjust strategy with initial capital
   150         1           72     72.0      0.0          self.strategy.adjust(self.initial_capital)
   151                                           
   152                                                   # loop through dates
   153     15382       243227     15.8      0.6          for dt in self.dates:
   154     15381     30341164   1972.6     72.0              self.strategy.update(dt)
   155     15381        16845      1.1      0.0              if not self.strategy.bankrupt:
   156     15381      3982239    258.9      9.5                  self.strategy.run()
   157                                                           # need update after to save weights, values and such
   158     15381      7133247    463.8     16.9                  self.strategy.update(dt)
   159                                           
   160         1       420509 420509.0      1.0          self.stats = self.strategy.prices.calc_perf_stats()
   161         1          193    193.0      0.0          self._original_prices = self.strategy.prices
```

If we [go deeper](https://gist.github.com/vfilimonov/59d0a6f274aa4ea956be) - then the bottlenecks of `.update()` are [pandas operations of setting a value](https://gist.github.com/vfilimonov/59d0a6f274aa4ea956be#file-gistfile1-txt-L87):
```
self._values[date] = val
```
and call of `c.update(date, data)` where [the same operations](https://gist.github.com/vfilimonov/59d0a6f274aa4ea956be#file-gistfile1-txt-L169) waste most of the time.

issue_title: `python-daemon-3K` normalized incorrectly
issue_body:
The index directory for package `python-daemon-3K` is normalized incorrectly to `python-daemon` rather than `python-daemon-3k`.

**Steps to reproduce**

```
$ pip2pi -nz foo/ python-daemon-3K==1.5.8
```

**Actual behavior**

```
$ ls -l foo/simple
[...]
drwxrwx--- 2 reidpr reidpr  80 Jul 21 11:28 python-daemon
[...]
```

`pip install` then fails with:

```
  DEPRECATION: Failed to find 'python-daemon-3K' at file:///[...]/packages/simple/python-daemon-3k/. It is suggested to upgrade your index to support normalized names as the name in /simple/{name}.
  Could not find a version that satisfies the requirement python-daemon-3K==1.5.8 (from -r requirements.txt (line 19)) (from versions: )
No matching distribution found for python-daemon-3K==1.5.8 (from -r requirements.txt (line 19))
```

**Expected behavior**

```
$ ls -l foo/simple
[...]
drwxrwx--- 2 reidpr reidpr  80 Jul 21 11:28 python-daemon-3k
[...]
```

**Comments**

* Manually re-naming the directory to append `-3k` makes it work.
* I suspect that `3K` is being erroneously interpreted as a version number.

issue_title: index order in match_events output
BedTool.filter silently ignoring exceptions
issue_body:
Here's an example that demonstrates the issue:

```python
In [76]: def foo(x):
    raise ValueError('foo')
   ....:

In [77]: list(bed.filter(foo))
Out[77]: []

In [78]: [x for x in bed if foo(x)]
---------------------------------------------------------------------------
ValueError                                Traceback (most recent call last)

<ipython-input-76-45cfbd08b10a> in foo(x)
      1 def foo(x):
----> 2     raise ValueError('foo')

```

As you can see, when using `filter` the exception is ignored somewhere along the way. I believe that the reason is that you're catching `ValueError` here: https://github.com/daler/pybedtools/blob/master/pybedtools/cbedtools.pyx#L760

issue_title: Spurious added = in completion candidates
issue_body:
Hello! I have seen a few weird cases where Jedi returns all possible completion candidates with an equals sign (`=`) as a suffix, apparently assuming those are possible keyword arguments to the called function. Sadly, this only happens in some files, and only sometimes – the same file can work perfectly fine usually, but break otherwise. I refrained from opening a bug report for a while due to this completely unhelpful description, but as I am now solving the problem by simply stripping equals signs, I figured I'd at least make you aware of this effect in case others report it as well.

(Only slightly) more information at jorgenschaefer/elpy#613

Typical example:

```Python
def foo(barbaz):
    return str(bar_|_
```

This will – under those unclear circumstances – provide a completion candidate of `barbaz=` instead of `barbaz`.

issue_title: Color-schemes are deprecated in each pywal release
issue_body:
Hi @dylanaraps I was taking a look at the new naming convention for the cache_file for each image, I noticed that with this way of differentiating the files, the users will lost all changes in a color-scheme with each new release of `pywal`, as they will be ignored and a new one will be generated, I have two suggestions that might help avoid this issue.

* handle template or color-scheme versions separately from `pywal`'s version,
so instead of appending `pywal.__version__` you could append something like `pywal.__template_version__`, which would only change each time there's a breaking change in the templates.

* be able to specify a custom cache_file name to `color.get` to allow `wpgtk` or other software that relies on `pywal` to decide it's own naming convention

I personally believe that my first suggestion is the best of the two and will please your users too, I'd do the PR myself, but I have no way of coding right now.

anyway, thanks for reading!

issue_title: Have not_valid_after and not_valid_before fields on x509.Certificate
issue_body:
We recently switched at androguard from cryptography to asn1crypto.
It looks like that the Certificate class are quite compatbile but it bugs me, that you can not easily get the validity of the certificate.
In cryptography's implementation, you have the fields `not_valid_after` and `not_valid_before` which return a datetime.
I learned you can do something like this:
```
not_valid_before = x509_cert['tbs_certificate']['validity']['not_before'].native
not_valid_after = x509_cert['tbs_certificate']['validity']['not_after'].native
```

It would be nice to have the same functionality as in cryptography library!

issue_title: Feature request: allow .append() to list instead of direct index assignment

```

    # do work
    pass

@synchronized
def populate_df(links):
    for i, link in enumerate(links):
        x[i] = scrape(link)
    return x
```

@concurrent
def scrape(link):

issue_title: number of producers and number of cores
issue_body:
Hi,

I would like to know whether there are any limits when the number of async producers is larger then the physical number of cores and each producer is running in its own process ?

we encounter that in such case not all the messages were sent to the kafka brokers.

***
I have the following use case:
I have created simple API using python Falcon framwork.
The api accept only GET requests (the source is mobile tracking service), extract url parameters from the request and send then as json message to kafka using async producer.
In order to scale the API to be able handling thousands of requests, I am using gunicorn.
I have tried gunicorn with 5 workers (when I have only 4 cores), thats mean that each worker is separate process, that will also create new kafka async producer.
In this configuration, we were not able to get all the messages using simple kafka consumer (cli).
We reduced then the number of workers to 2 and thats fine.

What could be the reason for not sending all the messages ?

my code
***

import falcon
from kafka import SimpleProducer, KafkaClient
import signal
import sys
import json
import os

import logging
import sys
logger = logging.getLogger('kafka')
lgr_frmt = logging.Formatter('%(asctime)s - %(name)s - %(levelname)s - %(message)s\n')
main_fh = logging.FileHandler("/tmp/kafka_{pid}.log".format(pid=str(os.getpid())), mode='a')
main_fh.setFormatter(lgr_frmt)
logger.setLevel(logging.DEBUG)
logger.addHandler(main_fh)

class CollectorApi():


    def __init__(self):
        # the signal is taking care of case when the app (or worker) are terminated by SIGINT or SIGTERM
        signal.signal(signal.SIGINT, self.signal_term_handler)
        signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, self.signal_term_handler)
        # supported os types
        self.supported_os_types = ('ios',)

    # template for kafka topics as function of os_type
    kafka_topic = "{os_type}"
    # create async kafka producer
    kafka = KafkaClient('kafka1:9092', 'kafka2:9092')
    producer = SimpleProducer(kafka, batch_send_every_n=50, batch_send_every_t=60, async=True)

    def signal_term_handler(self, signal, frame):
        """
        register signal handlers
        """
        self.producer.stop()
        sys.exit()

    def on_get(self, req, resp):
        #msg = req.relative_uri

        # parse event params and get the os type from the event
        event_params = req.params
        try:
            event_os_type = event_params['os']
        except KeyError:
            # if there is no os key in the params, then set os=unknown
            event_os_type = "unknown"
        else:
            # if the os type not in the supported os types list, set os=unknown
            if event_os_type not in self.supported_os_types:
                event_os_type = "unknown"

        # send to kafka only the params as json message.
        self.producer.send_messages(self.kafka_topic.format(os_type=event_os_type), json.dumps(event_params))


api = application = falcon.API()
collector = CollectorApi()
api.add_route('/', collector)

***

issue_title: Input from standard input doesn't work (on Linux)
issue_body:
I think that. in `__main.py__` the line:
            `win.set_dotcode(sys.stdin.read())`
should be:
           ` win.set_dotcode(sys.stdin.buffer.read())`
because it expects a byte string. See https://docs.python.org/3/library/sys.html#sys.stdin where the note says 

> To write or read binary data from/to the standard streams, use the underlying binary buffer object.

issue_title: CPU additionnal stats monitoring: Context switch, Interrupts...
issue_body:
PSUtil 4.1.0 introduces a new function to monitor the context switch (CS) (http://pythonhosted.org/psutil/#psutil.cpu_stats).

A context switch occurs when the kernel switches the processor from one thread to another—for example, when a thread with a higher priority than the running thread becomes ready. Context switching activity is important for several reasons. A program that monopolizes the processor lowers the rate of context switches because it does not allow much processor time for the other processes' threads. A high rate of context switching means that the processor is being shared repeatedly—for example, by many threads of equal priority. A high context-switch rate often indicates that there are too many threads competing for the processors on the system. (source https://technet.microsoft.com/en-us/library/cc938606.aspx).

The goal is to implement the monitoring of CPU CS into Glances:
- where ? CPU plugin ?
- optionnal ?
- limit rate ? (http://stackoverflow.com/questions/2794499/how-expensive-is-a-context-switch-is-it-better-to-implement-a-manual-task-switc)

Other resources:
- http://www.thomaskoetzing.de/index.php?option=com_content&task=view&id=196&Itemid=85

Expose 'after' callback methods
issue_body:
One very useful feature in Tkinter that's not currently exposed by `easygui` is [alarm events and non-event callbacks](http://effbot.org/tkinterbook/widget.htm). Particularly, the `after` widget method. Normally, when running a box; the box's mainloop blocks and no interaction can be made until an event (like a button press) is triggered. The `after` callback makes it easy for you to interact with the box during its mainloop.

Currently, to access this method, you'd have to know to start a box with `run=False` then access `box.ui.boxRoot.after` that way. I'm proposing that this simply be provided by a keyworded argument to box functions and their underlying classes. Such as `box = eg.buttonbox(msg=msg, after=(delay,my_func,args))`


Below is an example of how this callback can be used in the current version of easygui. I think many people will find it very useful. It would be nice if this feature were exposed and documented in easygui.

```
'''Quiz with a 10 second timeout'''

import easygui as eg
import time

BOX = eg.buttonbox(msg="", title="QUIZ!", choices=['1','2','3'], run=False)

def timeout_poll(start, timeout=10):
    expires = start + timeout
    time_remaining = expires - time.time()
    if time_remaining <= 0:
        try:
            BOX.ui.boxRoot.destroy()
        except:
            pass
        eg.msgbox("TIME IS UP!")
        return
    message = "What is 1 + 1? You have {} seconds to choose!\n{} seconds remaining".format(timeout, int(time_remaining))
    BOX.msg = message
    BOX.ui.boxRoot.after(100, timeout_poll, start)

start = time.time()
BOX.ui.boxRoot.after(0, timeout_poll, start)
try:
    choice = BOX.run()
except:
    #if box was destroyed
    choice = None

if choice == '2':
    eg.msgbox(msg="Correct!", title="QUIZ")
else:
    eg.msgbox(msg="Sorry, try again!", title="QUIZ!")
```

issue_title: Using custom properties / field codes
issue_body:
Hi !

In a Word document, it's possible to add field codes, with `ctrl+F9` or via QuickPart, and the content `{ DOCPROPERTY prop_name }`. Those [custom properties are manageable](https://support.office.com/en-US/article/View-or-change-the-properties-for-an-Office-file-21D604C2-481E-4379-8E54-1DD4622C6B75#viewcreate16) and this allows to use a docx as a template where variables can be easily replaced.

In a pptx file, [it's not possible to insert such fields](http://superuser.com/questions/142012/powerpoint-how-to-insert-document-property-aka-field-in-slide), even if it's possible to set properties, that are stored in `docProps/custom.xml`. They don't seem to be accessible from python-pptx.

Do you see a simple way to insert custom variable placeholders in a powerpoint, that python-pptx will be able to update easily without messing up formatting and position ?

For the moment, we iterate over all paragraphs in all shapes, and replace ``{{variable name}}`` that the author put especially there with the appropriate value, but it destroys the formatting when this variable is inside a sentence with multiple formattings (example: some words are underlined).

Thanks !

issue_title: TypeError while logging to file with --debug
issue_body:
Just a minor issue while logging debug info to a file I noticed this:

> 2016-10-05 19:07:50 WARNING Instance `netdata`, user method `net_speed` failed (TypeError) core.py line 497.

```
TypeError: write() argument must be str, not bytes
  File "/usr/lib/python3.5/site-packages/py3status/module.py", line 496, in run
    'method {} returned {} '.format(meth, result))
  File "/usr/lib/python3.5/site-packages/py3status/core.py", line 497, in log
    f.write(out.encode('utf-8'))
```

The unicode arrows used by the netdata module are causing the issue. I found adding `encoding='utf8'` to the open() in core.py log method fixes this issue for python3 if you don't mind the unprintable chars in your file.  Python2 needs io.open() to support the encoding parameter though. Since both versions can use io.open() I think it might the best way to avoid TypeError and UnicodeEncodeError for modules like this.

As a side note I'm not sure how good or bad writing utf8 to a file is since open() uses the filesystem encoding setting as the default.  Its different than the default encoding on my system at least.
```
>>> sys.getdefaultencoding()
'utf-8'
>>> sys.getfilesystemencoding()
'ascii'
```
So instead of encoding='utf8' in io.open() if you just use errors='backslashreplace' you will have nice ascii printable unicode escape codes in your file.

If you think the solution is good I can create another pull request.

issue_title: OpenBSD file: unknown option -- mime-encoding
issue_body:
On OpenBSD (6.2-current), py3status fails with an error:
```
file: unknown option -- mime-encoding
usage: file [-bchiLsW] file ...
```
The `file` command on OpenBSD does not have the `--mime-encoding` option. The command is used in: `parse_config.py` ine 536
```
encoding = check_output(['file', '-b', '--mime-encoding', '--dereference', config_path])
encoding = encoding.strip().decode('utf-8')
```
As a workaround, I replaced those lines with hardcoded value:
```
encoding = 'utf-8'
```
then the program runs OK.

Maybe encoding guess should use a python library (chardet?) instead of using the file command. I will try to propose a patch (never coded anything in python, but this is a nice way to learn).

issue_title: Update Cython to latest
issue_body:
A thorough testing will be required, as version 0.20 breaks some things. While 
doing a quick testing, one issue was found so far.
## Issue with strings

The Debug() function needs to accept both unicode and bytes strings. In version 0.19.2 of Cython you could pass unicode string and it was converted automatically, but with the new version an error is throwed:

```
 TypeError: Expected str, got unicode
```

The Debug() function:
https://code.google.com/p/cefpython/source/browse/cefpython/utils.pyx?r=2f6f611c2fcf#20

Called from CreateJavascriptCallback():
https://code.google.com/p/cefpython/source/browse/cefpython/javascript_callback_cef3.pyx?r=2f6f611c2fcf

The functionName param was decoded from the cefpython IPC message using the json library, and the json library automatically converted the string to unicode.

There might be more places where unicode is passed and it would throw an error.

issue_title: Security risks of the default settings
issue_body:
The default `IPWARE_META_PRECEDENCE_ORDER` settings cause django-ipware to check for various types of headers by default. However, not all headers may be there, or get filtered through the webserver.

In a standard setup (apache+mod_wsgi or nginx+uwsgi), only `REMOTE_ADDR` applies and needs to be read. Since there are many other headers in `IPWARE_META_PRECEDENCE_ORDER`, I could pass any of these to fake an IP address:
* `X-Forwarded-For`
* `Client-IP`
* `X-Real-IP`
* `X-Forwarded`
* `X-Cluster-Client-IP`
* `Forwarded-For`
* `Forwarded`
* `Via`

I'd strongly recommend reducing `IPWARE_META_PRECEDENCE_ORDER` to just `REMOTE_ADDR` and let users configure their system explicitly when they use a forwarding http server in their setup.

issue_title: troubles controlling PMP navigation, playMedia and goToMedia
issue_body:
Hi all.

First of all, a big thank-you to all that have contributed to this python API.  I'm am trying to learn how to use the Plex API and without this project I think it would be impossible for me because the Plex API documents are so incomplete.  In general I'm finding the plex API quite frustrating and unpredictable.  This is no reflection on the Python API of course.

I'm using PMP (Plex Media Player) on Windows as my player.  Plex server is also running on a Windows machine.

I'm having issues with navigation, playMedia and goToMedia.  Some things only work when PMP is in desktop mode and some only work when PMP is in TV Full Screen mode.  And then some don't work at all.

I'm just wondering if you all are experiencing the same thing.  In some cases I think it's just that Plex does not actually support those things, but in some cases I can get things to work if I use the Plex browser in chrome to control PMP so they must be doing something differently from what this Python api is doing.

So here's what I've found and I'd appreciate any feedback on this from anyone here.

Navigation
- Does not work at all when PMP is in desktop mode.  But some things do work in TV Full Screen mode. 
- In TV Full Screen mode these work fine: select(), moveUp(), moveDown() etc., goToHome(), goBack().
- These don't work at all ever: pageDown(), goToMusic(), nextLetter() etc.

Now the really weird thing is with playMedia() and goToMedia():
- PMP in desktop mode: playMedia() works and goToMedia() does **not** work.
- PMP in TV Full Screen mode: playMedia() does **not** work **(times out**) and goToMedia() does work.

interestingly with PMP in TV Full Screen mode: goToMedia() does work when I use Chrome to control the PMP through "companion mode" or "mirroring" or whatever it's called.

Thanks in advance for any thoughts or guidance.

I'm trying to develop a plugin to control PMP from VoxCommando and if I can figure these things out it would help me a lot.  Once I get something working from your Python api I can adapt it to use in VoxCommando quite easily.

issue_title: AuthorizeInvalidError for amounts greater than $20,000
issue_body:
Amount SchemaNodes have an arbitrary and hardcoded max value of 20000 for apparently no reason, preventing transactions for higher values from going through.

The following code causes that error:
```python
import authorize

authorize.Configuration.configure(
    authorize.Environment.TEST,
    'api_login_id',
    'api_transaction_key',
)

result = authorize.Transaction.sale({
    'amount': 20001.00,
    'credit_card': {
        'card_number': '4111111111111111',
        'expiration_date': '04/2019',
        'card_code': '343',
    }
})
```

The error stems from the **schemas.py** file. Removing the max values of 20000 (there are others) fixes this, but I'm wondering if there's a reason for having those hardcoded values there (Authorize.net claims to not impose any upper limits on transaction amounts).

Thanks!

issue_title: Django Migrations Broken
issue_body:
#916 changed the app name from "default" to "social_auth". Users running the app prior this change will have migrations registered under the old name, resulting in Django thinking it should apply migrations from scratch.

issue_title: Dynamic Secret
issue_body:
Something I came across the other day that I really like is per-user secrets for signing JWTs. It is (according to me) a great way to manage validation of dynamic JWTs.

Example:
You want to store a user's permissions in the JWT. This could be useful for the front-end so that it knows what to render for each user. The issue? Permissions can change frequently (and often server-side, such as when banning a user). The most automatic solution is to sign the JWT with a hash of `the secret + all of the dynamic data`. That way, the JWT will automatically fail to validate if the user's permissions change.

Another common use-case is if the user changes the password and you want to invalidate all the old JWTs. Solution? Sign the JWT with `the secret + the user password hash`.

Now, one could argue that this requires a DB query and that it defeats the purpose of JWTs, but geez that argument is getting old. It is not the sole purpose of JWTs to prevent DB queries.

What do you think? I could submit a PR for this (or at least try) if you just point the way in terms of naming. I think having a loader, e.g. `secret_loader`, is the smoothest solution here. It should take the verified/unverified claims as a parameter and return a string that the JWT should be validated/signed with (aka a secret). It would run right before encoding and decoding a token. Alternatively, you have a loader for the decoding, and then just add a secret param to the create_access_token function.

issue_title: Windows path fix: should do like Django, not like Windows
issue_body:
In March I was in a hurry and I locally fixed a path issue where Django always worked with '/' but db_file_storage worked with os.sep (I fixed it locally because it was above the @sp-niemand fork that fixed Django 1.9, so contributing the fix was not easy anyway.)

This is my fix:

https://github.com/SonOfLilit/db_file_storage/commits/master

I recently noticed that the issue was fixed upstream (as well as the Django 1.9 support that was blocking me from using the official release):

https://github.com/victor-o-silva/db_file_storage/pull/28

However, when I tested the new version, I found that the official fix converted Django's forward slashes to backslashes on Windows, instead of using forward slashes internally on Windows too like Django does, and *like a db-based storage plugin should*, so I'm stuck on my unofficial version.

The abstract issue is that there is no relation between a storage plugin and the operating system, so why should the storage plugin introduce a portability issue between operating systems, or depend on the operating system for it's API/ABI in any way?

An example concrete issue is that I can't clone the production DB and work on it from a Windows machine. Few people ever host production Django systems on a Windows server [citation needed], so dev environments for systems that are hosted on Unix in production is the major use case for using Django on Windows, and therefore this matters.

I propose moving to my fix and creating a migration that converts all existing paths in the DB to forward slashes (perhaps only do it if there are slashes in the config part of the stored paths... Windows does not allow backslashes in a filename, does Unix?).

I can write the code if you like the idea and are willing to do Code Review.

Aur

issue_title: Support for languages per country (per model instance)
issue_body:
#### I have this special use case :

- **Multiple countries**

```python
# settings.py

COUNTRY_NAMES = {
	'ch': 'Switzerland',
	'fr': 'France',
	'jp': 'Japan',
}
```

- **Each having their own languages**  

```python
# settings.py

COUNTRY_LANGUAGES = {
	'ch': ('fr-ch', 'it-ch', 'de-ch', 'en-us'),
	'fr': ('fr-fr', 'en-us'),
	'jp': ('ja-jp', 'en-us'),
}

LANGUAGE_NAMES = {
	'de-ch': 'Deutsche',
	'en-us': 'English',
	'fr-ch': 'Français',
	'fr-fr': 'Français',
	'it-ch': 'Italiano',
	'ja-jp': '日本語',
}
```

- **settings.LANGUAGES** contains all possible languages

```python
# settings.py

LANGUAGES = LANGUAGE_NAMES.items()
```

- A **CountrySpecific** model mixin, so that inherited models belong to a specified country

```python
class CountrySpecific(models.Model):
	
	country_code = models.CharField('Country', max_length=2,
		choices=settings.COUNTRY_NAMES.items(),
	)
	
	class Meta:
		abstract = True
```

- **Each user belongs to a country**  
My custom `User` class inherit from `CountrySpecific`.

#### What I need is :

In the admin site, users should be able to edit `CountrySpecific` models in their languages only.

#### With modeltranslation :

If I use it the standard way :

```python
class Collection(CountrySpecific):
	title = models.CharField(max_length=255)

class CollectionTranslationOptions(TranslationOptions):
	fields = ('title',)

translator.register(Collection, CollectionTranslationOptions)
```

My `Collection` instances have translated fields for all possible languages, as specified in `settings.LANGUAGES`, and all translatable fields are visible in the admin site.

P.S. I'm running [Djangae](https://potatolondon.github.io/djangae/) on Google's NoSQL Datastore so I would't even need all translatable fields in the database, but I guess we have no choice because modeltranslation hack the model classes.

Anyway, I need at least the admin site with each user able to edit models in the languages based on their country only.

#### The hacked solution I did :

I noticed modeltranslation always loop on `modeltranslation.settings.AVAILABLE_LANGUAGES`.
So I made my own `TranslationAdmin` class, and I overrode all methods this way :  

```python
class MyTranslationAdmin(MethodWrappable, TranslationAdmin):
	
	_country_languages = None
	
	def _wrap_method(self, name, method, *args, **kwargs):
		
		if args and isinstance(args[0], HttpRequest):
			user = args[0].user
			self._country_languages = list(settings.COUNTRY_LANGUAGES[user.country_code])
		
		if self._country_languages:
			mt_lang_path = 'modeltranslation.settings.AVAILABLE_LANGUAGES'
			with sleuth.switch(mt_lang_path, self._country_languages):
				return method(*args, **kwargs)
		else:
			return method(*args, **kwargs)
```

With `MethodWrappable` a utility mixin I did to override/wrap class methods, and `sleuth`, [this mocking library](https://github.com/Kazade/sleuth).

This is a brute force hack, I confess! :stuck_out_tongue:
But this way, I can do what I need :
Define the translated field languages based on a `request` obj.

Do you have a better idea?
Or plan to support that in the future?

I saw you have a `get_translation_field_excludes(self, exclude_languages=None)` method in `TranslationBaseModelAdmin` with a TODO note : `TODO: Currently unused?`.

issue_title: Prevent detection of imports in comments (SCSS)
issue_body:
Bootstrap 4 currently contains an import in a comment (https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/1a6d0cc/scss/_reboot.scss#L47) that would create an infinite loop if it were outside a comment. Since the SCSS dependency detection ignores comments, it detects the import even though it shouldn't and thus creates the infinite loop: bootstrap.scss -> _reboot.scss -> bootstrap.scss -> …

The SCSS compiler needs to handle comments and ignore everything in there to prevent this bug in the future.

issue_title: Daphne logger(s) not set up to handle error output
issue_body:
I have a Twisted Looping call that pumps out the current time (from a Django app).

If I set that Looping call so it's sending out a bad key (before it's ever called), then load the page that causes that Looping call to start, the first time it tries to send, I get this:

[2016/09/11 16:21:27] WebSocket CONNECT /liveblog/ssteinerx-live-blog/stream/ [127.0.0.1:50558]
('Trying to create channel for:', u'ssteinerx-live-blog')
[2016/09/11 16:21:27] WebSocket CONNECT /update_time/stream/ [127.0.0.1:50560]
('Adding update_time group...', <channels.channel.Channel object at 0x000000010a7ca330>)
{'time': '2016-09-11 16:21:27.696304'}
LoopingCall started...
No handlers could be found for logger "daphne.server"
{'time': '2016-09-11 16:21:28.697522'}
This is 100% repeatable. Yay. If the logger is set up and ready to roll on the next bad call, I don't know, but the "No handlers..." message does not repeat.

There is no handler for the logger's `error()` call.

So, this:

Python 2.7.10 (0e2d9a73f5a1818d0245d75daccdbe21b2d5c3ef, Sep 07 2016, 19:23:10)
[PyPy 5.4.1 with GCC 4.2.1 Compatible Apple LLVM 7.3.0 (clang-703.0.31)] on darwin
Type "help", "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information.
>>>> import logging
>>>> l = logging.getLogger(__name__)
>>>> l.debug("Debugooy")
>>>> l.info("Infoooy")
>>>> l.warn("Warnooy")
>>>> l.error("Ahfooey")
No handlers could be found for logger "__main__"

The reason the message about the handler is coming out at all [logging doc on this](https://docs.python.org/2/howto/logging.html#what-happens-if-no-configuration-is-provided

The error() handler needs to be configured with destination for the logger.error in server.py (and probably elsewhere as well). Not sure how you want it handled, but it has to be explicitly handled. The error message only appears once, but I'm pretty sure the rest of those logger.error() calls are just falling on the floor, so they need to be hooked up to something. Who knew?

issue_title: Variable batch size (number of samples, the dim1) during forward-backward computation
issue_body:
Hello,

I am working on an architecture which has such a mechanism: Imagine a data batch with N samples. Each sample can have an arbitrary shape. For example, the shape can be 4D (N,a,b,c), the result of a convolution operation or 2D (N,m), the result of a Fully Connected layer. I have an operation, call it F, when applied to an input batch, it determines that n of the N samples in the batch won't be processed by the network further. So, only N-n samples should be propagated through F. The next operation should operate only on N-n samples.  

For example: We place F after a convolution operation. It takes a (N,a,b,c) shaped batch as the input. F determines that n of the samples will be dropped. It selects the N-n relevant samples, packs them into a (N-n,a,b,c) shaped batch and propagates them into the next layer. It should be noted that this is different than Dropout, F deterministically decides which samples to drop.

I am currently able to do that by supressing the dropped samples by using workarounds like multiplying them by zero, blocking their gradients etc. But this leads to a lot of redundant processing time for samples which we won't use and slows down the training immensely.

I wonder if it is possible to build a custom operator which drops such samples online and transfers only the relevant samples to the next layers? I see that each operator needs to implement an infer_shape function, and declares what their input and output shapes will be in advance. In my case, the output shape will be determined as the result of the operator's computation, so I have doubts that this could be implemented straightforwardly. What would you suggest in this case? Does this need a heavy modification of the underlying MxNet library for example? Or could it be implemented by simple CustomOperators somehow?

Thanks.

issue_title: gluon.data.vision datasets should have layout option
issue_body:
(mxnet 1.0.0)

The datasets provided in mxnet.gluon.data.vision do not provide an option for the initial data shape and simply put the channel dimension last for some reason. Unfortunately, this choice clashes with the default layout of NCHW for the Conv2D block, which is frequently the first layer in models using these data sets. It also clashes with the native layout of these datasets. In the case of CIFAR, because there is more than one channel, it has to actually reorder all of the data only to have it reordered back again. What is the rationale for putting the channel last? 

There should be an option independent of the transform keyword to specify the initial layout of the dataset, at least with respect to the location of channel dimension, e.g.

~~~python
dataset = MNIST(layout='NCHW', transform=my_transform)
~~~

It would probably be sufficient to only implement layouts 'NCHW' and 'NHWC'.

Note that reshaping the transform function is not as easy as it might seem since you may need to handle varying batch sizes or even a dropped dimension in the case where you take a single element slice of the dataset. 

For instance, here is the transform I used that was intended to be usable with both MNIST and CIFAR with varying batch sizes:

~~~python
        def data_transform(data:NDArray, label:NDArray) -> (NDArray, NDArray):
            shape = data.shape
            if shape[-1] == 1:
                # If there is only one channel, data is already correctly ordered
                # and we can just reshape.
                data2 = data.reshape(shape[:-3] + (1,) + shape[-3:-1])
            elif len(shape) == 4:
                # Otherwise we need to transpose
                data2 = data.transpose((0,3,1,2))
            else:
                assert len(shape) == 3
                data2 = data.transpose((2,0,1))

            return data2.astype(self.dtype) * pixel_scale + pixel_offset, label
~~~

If the underlying dataset had the channel in the right position, the transform could just be a one liner.

issue_title: Under Python 2.7/3.5, whl depends upon cryptography<1.9
issue_body:
Still investigating, but my guess is that the whl is being built with Python 3.2 or Python 3.3, causing the whl's METADATA to pin to the old version of crypotography (1.8.2), but the latest PyOpenSSL (17.5.0) requires cryptography>=2.1.4.  This is causing an issue for me due to a custom setup.py `console_scripts`, since `pkg_resources.load_entry_point` verifies the version compatibility and mozilla_django_oidc, and it's direct crypotography dependency conflicts with its transitive crypotography via PyOpenSSL.

You can download the mozilla_django_oidc from pypi and examine the METADATA file:

    Requires-Dist: cryptography (<1.9)

Contradictory to the [intent of setup.py](https://github.com/mozilla/mozilla-django-oidc/blob/b79015f0e71d04fb38ff9fcc54bbf2a1a165f8a5/setup.py#L41), you can see that under python 3.4, cryptography==1.8.2 is downloaded (same result for python 2.7):

    $ virtualenv --no-site-packages --python=python3.4 env
    $ env/bin/pip install mozilla_django_oidc==0.4.2
    ...
    Downloading/unpacking cryptography<1.9 (from mozilla-django-oidc==0.4.2)
      Downloading cryptography-1.8.2.tar.gz (423kB): 423kB downloaded
    ...

Installing from source under python3.4 or python2.7 works as intended:

    $ git clone https://github.com/mozilla/mozilla-django-oidc.git
    $ cd mozilla-django-oidc
    $ virtualenv --no-site-packages --python=python3.4 env
    $ env/bin/python3.4 setup.py install
    ...
    Processing dependencies for mozilla-django-oidc==0.4.2
    Searching for cryptography>1.9
    Reading https://pypi.python.org/simple/cryptography/
    Best match: cryptography 2.1.4
    ...

I have been trying to come up with a PR with suggested fix, but don't feel like I have a great idea yet.  Easiest solution seems to be to build whl with Python3.4+ or 2.7 (I'm assuming it was built with 3.2/3.3, but maybe I'm wrong), however that would break the whl for 3.2/3.3 users (is this a required target still?).  Turning off the universal flag for the wheel, and building per-version via `--python-tag` may be an option.

issue_title: python-http-client should define it's own error class which can be imported by consuming libraries
issue_body:
#### Issue Summary

If the response is not success (i.e not HTTP2xx) the library throws a urllib HTTPError.
For clients consuming this library, they now need to know that it was implemented in urllib, import that module into their application and handle the error.

Ideally, a consuming application only needs to know about python-http-client and not how urllib was implemented in order to handle common place http errors.

#### Steps to Reproduce

1. make an api call that returns anything other than HTTP2xx
2. attempt to handle error raised without importing urllib and without doing a catch all.


#### Technical details:

* python-http-client Version: master (latest commit: [commit number])
* Python Version: all

issue_title: Parameter containing unicode chars fail to serialize (MS Windows)
issue_body:
## Issue
A parameter set to contain unicode characters will make luigi fail with a UnicodeEncodeError on Windows 7.

## Root cause
Luigi serializes parameters by using the `str` function instead of `unicode`.

## Fix
In file `luigi/parameter.py`, class `Parameter`, function `serialize`, replace:

	> return str(x)
	
With:

	> return unicode(x)
	
## Steps to reproduce

Create a file named "test.py" filled with:

	> # -*- coding: iso-8859-1 -*-
	> 
	> import luigi
	> 
	> class UnicodeTask (luigi.WrapperTask):
	> 	unicode_param = luigi.Parameter(u"é")
	> 	
	> if __name__ == '__main__':
	>     luigi.run(main_task_cls=UnicodeTask)
	
Open the Windows command line (CMD.EXE) and run:

	> python test.py --local-scheduler

issue_title: Security group authorize_* methods UserIdGroupPairs GroupName only uses default VPC
issue_body:
The security group authorize_ingress and authorize_egress methods with a specified UserIdGroupPairs GroupName only look for that GroupName in the default VPC instead of looking in the VPC of the calling security group resource. Also, specifying the UserIdGroupPairs VpcId does not have any effect.

For example:
```
> s = boto3.Session()
> ec2 = s.resource('ec2')
> vpc1 = ec2.Vpc(id='vpc-xxxxxxx1') #default VPC
> vpc2 = ec2.Vpc(id='vpc-xxxxxxx2') #VPC2
> sg1_vpc2 = ec2.SecurityGroup(id='sg-xxxxxxx1') #security group in VPC2
> new_rule1 =
{'FromPort': 25,
 'IpProtocol': 'tcp',
 'IpRanges': [],
 'PrefixListIds': [],
 'ToPort': 25,
 'UserIdGroupPairs': [{'GroupId': 'sg-xxxxxxx2'}]} #id in VPC2
> sg1_vpc2.authorize_ingress(IpPermissions=[new_rule1]) #by id -> works
> new_rule2 = 
{'FromPort': 25,
 'IpProtocol': 'tcp',
 'IpRanges': [],
 'PrefixListIds': [],
 'ToPort': 25,
 'UserIdGroupPairs': [{'GroupName': 'sg2_vpc2'}]}
> sg1_vpc2.authorize_ingress(IpPermissions=[new_rule2]) #by group name
...
ClientError: An error occurred (InvalidGroup.NotFound) when calling the AuthorizeSecurityGroupIngress operation: You have specified two resources that belong to different networks.
# Error -> looking for 'sg2_vpc2' in the default vpc instead of VPC2
> new_rule3 = 
{'FromPort': 25,
 'IpProtocol': 'tcp',
 'IpRanges': [],
 'PrefixListIds': [],
 'ToPort': 25,
 'UserIdGroupPairs': [{'GroupName': 'sg2_vpc2', 'VpcId': 'vpc-xxxxxxx2'}]}
> sg1_vpc2.authorize_ingress(IpPermissions=[new_rule3]) #by group name with explicit VpcId
...
ClientError: An error occurred (InvalidGroup.NotFound) when calling the AuthorizeSecurityGroupIngress operation: You have specified two resources that belong to different networks.
# Error -> looking for 'sg2_vpc2' in the default vpc instead of VPC2
```

issue_title: Can't access outer iframe after accessing nested iframe in context manager
issue_body:
Hi,

I'm trying to use Splinter automate something on a site that's structured something like this:

```
top level page
| iframe
| | iframe
| | | form widgets
| | submit button
```

(This is exactly as fun as it looks, by the way.)

If I write my code like this:

``` python
    with browser.get_iframe('outerframe') as outer_iframe:
        with outer_iframe.get_iframe('innerframe') as iframe:
            iframe.find_by_id('field1').first.fill('value')
            iframe.find_by_id('field2').first.fill('value')

        outer_iframe.find_by_id('button').first.click()
```

I get an exception like this:

```
splinter.exceptions.ElementDoesNotExist: no elements could be found with id "button"
```

But if I write my code like this, it works:

``` python
    with browser.get_iframe('outerframe') as outer_iframe:
        with outer_iframe.get_iframe('innerframe') as iframe:
            iframe.find_by_id('field1').first.fill('value')
            iframe.find_by_id('field2').first.fill('value')

    with browser.get_iframe('outerframe') as outer_iframe:
        outer_iframe.find_by_id('button').first.click()
```

(As an aside, Splinter is amazing, it's made my life a lot easier today, thanks!)

issue_title: Timestamp and datetime conversions may crash on Windows
issue_body:
Similar to https://github.com/biolab/orange3/issues/2828 .

##### Timeseries version
Since `datetime.fromtimestamp` and `dt.timestamp()` where `dt` is `datetime` is used.


##### Expected behavior
Function works on any platform.


##### Actual behavior
- `datetime.fromtimestamp(ts)` where `ts < 0`: `OSError` 
- `dt.timestamp()` where `dt.year < 1970`: `OverflowError`

##### Steps to reproduce the behavior



##### Additional info (worksheets, data, screenshots, ...)
`OverflowError`: https://sentry.io/biolab/orange3-timeseries/issues/428467601/
`OSError`: https://sentry.io/biolab/orange3-timeseries/issues/393933243/

issue_title: Make a licensing note somewhere about `pipenv check`
issue_body:
While writing https://github.com/pyupio/safety-db/pull/2261, I realised that commercial redistributors of `pipenv` should take close note of the fact that `pipenv check` relies on a CC-BY-NC-SA CVE database maintained by pyup.io: https://github.com/pyupio/safety-db

This means that commercial redistributors of `pipenv` need to choose between:

* disabling `pipenv check`;
* patching `pipenv` to use a different vulnerability database (e.g. one they maintain themselves); or
* paying `pyup.io` for a commercial usage license

issue_title: `test_save_nothing (resources.test_updateable.UpdateableAPIResourceTests)` is failing
skimage.external.tifffile fails to use the compiled tifffile extension
issue_body:
**Update:** As noted below, #1549 fixes the original problem described here, but we've run into a second issue which also prevents the `_tifffile.so` extension from loading properly. Explanation below.

----

The version of `tifffile.py` included in `skimage.external` is slightly out-of-date with respect to the [version on the author's website](http://www.lfd.uci.edu/~gohlke/code/tifffile.py.html).

Critically, the version in scikit-image includes a typo in the [`_replace_by`](https://github.com/scikit-image/scikit-image/blob/1b38c172800b45c04fcf958c4e8712ae7374ea8a/skimage/external/tifffile/tifffile_local.py#L3117) function, which prevents the C-extension code in `_tifffile.so` from getting imported.  Thus, although the `_tifffile.so` C-extension is built properly, it isn't ever used.

Fortunately, a pure-python implementation of `decodelzw()` exists as a fallback. Unfortunately, it is an order of magnitude slower than the C implementation.

issue_title: Add a "checkInputs" parameter to TemporalMemory
issue_body:
Here's the current state of things:

- Encoders output **a binary array**.
- The SpatialPooler outputs **a binary array**.
- The TemporalMemory receives **a list of active column indices**.
- Binary arrays and lists of indices are identical types. They're vectors of numbers.
- If you give the TemporalMemory a binary array, it doesn't complain, it just quietly does the wrong thing.

I've been bitten by this before, and so have others. By default, the TemporalMemory should check that the input vector is valid. It should offer a "fast mode" option which disables this check.

Today, a valid TemporalMemory input:

- Contains no duplicates

In the future, a valid TemporalMemory input also might need to be sorted, but that's not required today. _(We'd be careful to find a backward-compatible way to do this.)_ Also, some research code already expects external / apical inputs to be sorted. It will be good for our research productivity to have this runtime check.

Proposal: Let's add a "checkInputs" parameter which enables these checks. It will default to True in the constructor.

issue_title: Override check result in case of 'consider'
issue_body:
When a check is complete, it can have one of two results - 'clear', which means all reports came back clear, and 'consider', which means that one or more of them did not. In this it is up to the users of the lib to decide whether to pass the check or not. e.g. if an identity check fails because an uploaded passport photo is bad, that doesn't mean they fail the test, just that it can't pass automatically. However, if you are restricting someone's use of your app because of their check status, this poses a problem:

```
if check.result != 'clear':
    print "You cannot proceed"
```

Obviously you could just overwrite the result with 'clear' but you are then amending the history, and this is not ideal. In the event of an audit you need to be able to demonstrate what happened. Alternative is an additional field that can be used to declare the check as clear, outside of the callbacks. I think this is my preferred option - a NullBooleanField that is None by default, set to True automatically if a check is updated via the API to 'clear', but then overridable in the admin.

issue_title: MKL issues
issue_body:
I am using Intel compiler version 14.0.2 with mkl v11.1.2 on intel64 architecture.

There are a few issues with the default mkl_libs in site.cfg.example

* First, `mkl_blas95_lp64` and `mkl_lapack95_lp64` are only static (`.a`) and I must compile shared object (I believe this is true for everybody). So I took them out.

* Second, `libmkl_core.so` is not complete as the `libmkl_core.a` and therefore there will be some undefined symbols. Those are in `libmkl_def.so` instead. So I added the `mkl_def`.

* Third, compiling this way succeeds, but fails at runtime with:
`ImportError: /opt/intel/mkl/lib/intel64/libmkl_avx.so: undefined symbol: ownLastTriangle_64f`
(which, for the record, is very close to this https://software.intel.com/en-us/forums/topic/473447 related problem)
`nm /opt/intel/*/lib/intel64/*.so | grep " ownLastTriangle_64" # notice the space before "own"`
does find many `U` (usage) of `ownLastTriangle_64`s but no `T` (text definition). Dropping the space in the above grep, does find some `T` but all prefixed and not resolved automatically, short of symbol mangling. 
Therefore, I took mkl_avx out too.

In conclusion, this is my working libs line in site.cfg:
`mkl_libs = mkl_intel_lp64, mkl_gf_lp64, mkl_intel_thread, mkl_core, mkl_def, mkl_vml_avx, mkl_rt, iomp5`

PS: writing this as issue instead of pull request since I suspect different installs of the compiler and the mkl may need different things -- and obviously the problem is solved for me, so you can close it right away (possibly including a comment in the site.cfg.example)

issue_title: A better url builder
issue_body:
Hello,

I noticed that each data reader have it's how way to build URL.

That's sometimes very ugly.

For example there is things like (`yahoo/components.py`)

    idx_mod = idx_sym.replace('^', '@%5E')

the use of `urlencode` should probably be prefered

pylint doesn't like in `yahoo/actions.py` (tabulations)

    url = (_URL + 's=%s' % symbol + \
                '&a=%s' % (start.month - 1) + \
                '&b=%s' % start.day + \
                '&c=%s' % start.year + \
                '&d=%s' % (end.month - 1) + \
                '&e=%s' % end.day + \
                '&f=%s' % end.year + \
                '&g=v')

and many others...

Maybe we should add in `utils` a function to build url from a base URL (and endpoint) and a dictionary of parameters and encourage each data reader developer to use it.

Such a function could be

    def _encode_url(url, params):
        """
        Return encoded url with parameters
        """
        s_params = urlencode(params)
        if s_params!=0:
            return url + '?' + s_params
        else:
            return url

So in `google/daily.py` we could have

    params = {
        'q': sym,
        'startdate': start.strftime('%b %d, %Y'),
        'enddate': end.strftime('%b %d, %Y'),
        'output': "csv"
    }
    url = _encode_url(_URL, params)
    return _retry_read_url(url, retry_count, pause, 'Google')

instead of

    url = "%s%s" % (_URL,
                    urlencode({"q": sym,
                               "startdate": start.strftime('%b %d, ' '%Y'),
                               "enddate": end.strftime('%b %d, %Y'),
                               "output": "csv"}))

or in `yahoo/daily.py` instead of

    url = (_URL + 's=%s' % sym +
           '&a=%s' % (start.month - 1) +
           '&b=%s' % start.day +
           '&c=%s' % start.year +
           '&d=%s' % (end.month - 1) +
           '&e=%s' % end.day +
           '&f=%s' % end.year +
           '&g=%s' % interval +
           '&ignore=.csv')

we could have

    params = {
        's': sym,
        'a': start.month - 1,
        'b': start.day,
        'c': start.year,
        'd': end.month - 1,
        'e': end.day,
        'f': end.year,
        'g': interval,
        'ignore': '.csv'
    }
    url = _encode_url(_URL, params)


WorldBank API url create could also be improved:

in `_get_data`, instead of

    url = ("http://api.worldbank.org/countries/" + countries + "/indicators/" +
           indicator + "?date=" + str(start) + ":" + str(end) +
           "&per_page=25000&format=json")

"http://api.worldbank.org" is often repeat

in such a url there is 3 parts:

base url: "http://api.worldbank.org"
endpoint: "/countries/US/indicators/NY.GNS.ICTR.GN.ZS" (for example)
parameters: date=2012:2015&per_page=25000&format=json

We shouldn't repeat ourself
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Don%27t_repeat_yourself

we could have

global

    _BASE_URL = 'http://api.worldbank.org'

in `get_data`

        endpoint = "/countries/{countries}/indicators/{indicator}"\
            .format(countries=countries, indicator=indicator)
        params = {
            'date': "%s:%s" % (start, end),
            'per_page': 25000,
            'format': 'json'
        }
        url = _encode_url(_BASE_URL + endpoint, params)

Maybe we might define how data reader "should" build URLs.

Kind regards

issue_title: Don't delete QtCore.{pyqtSignal,pyqtSlot,pyqtProperty}
issue_body:
The [README says](https://github.com/spyder-ide/qtpy/blob/8f4207d88e4c83e71890ab54d0c80179c19b74ce/README.md#description) the API is [PyQt5 compatible](http://pyqt.sourceforge.net/Docs/PyQt5/signals_slots.html#defining-new-signals-with-pyqtsignal), yet the PyQt5 identifiers such as `QtCore.pyqtSignal` are [explicitly deleted](https://github.com/spyder-ide/qtpy/blob/31dbc9eb01329af3d7d67cbfd6ee76a632aa84c2/qtpy/QtCore.py#L24) in favor of PySide's.

PyQt5's symbols were removed in https://github.com/spyder-ide/qtpy/pull/47 with IMHO not so great justification:

> I still think that the duplicate imports are very weird and not needed

issue_title: Bug: paths in role ARNs aren't supported
issue_body:
I believe there is a bug in this client where paths in role ARNs aren't working.

Basically, if you have an instance-profile ARN like: `arn:aws:iam::1234567890123:instance-profile/foobar/foo/foo-app`

The role ARN needs to be contructed to look like this: `arn:aws:iam::1234567890123:role/foobar/foo/foo-app`

We had the same issue in the java client and the fix can be found in how we generate role ARNs from the metadata endpoints in this class,
https://github.com/Nike-Inc/cerberus-java-client/blob/master/src/main/java/com/nike/cerberus/client/auth/aws/InstanceRoleVaultCredentialsProvider.java

See method buildIamRoleArns() and the args for that method should be gathered from the two endpoints:
1. EC2MetadataUtils.getIAMInstanceProfileInfo().instanceProfileArn; e.g. http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/iam/info
2. EC2MetadataUtils.getIAMSecurityCredentials().keySet(); e.g. http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/iam/security-credentials/

Above handles the pathing edge case as well as another that occurs in CloudFormation where the role name doesn't match the instance profile name.

issue_title: Short option autocompleted with an extra equal sign (Bash)
issue_body:
Here is my test program:

```shell
Usage:
  #{__FILE__} [--test=<test-value>]
  #{__FILE__} -h | --help | --version

Options:
  -h, --help               Shows this screen.
  -v, --version            Display the program version.
  -t, --test <test-value>  My own test value
```

Here are some use-cases for the above program (I'm using `docopt.rb` here):

```shell
# Valid case: long option with equals sign
./docopt-test.rb --test=1234

# Output
{"--test"=>"1234", "--help"=>false, "--version"=>false}

# Valid case: short option with a space
./docopt-test.rb -t 1234

# Output
{"--test"=>"1234", "--help"=>false, "--version"=>false}

# Invalid case: short option with an equal sign
./docopt-test.rb -t=1234

# Output (the value is =1234 here)
{"--test"=>"=1234", "--help"=>false, "--version"=>false}
```

Here is the autocomplete file I get when using this tool:

```shell
_docopttestrb()
{
    local cur
    cur="${COMP_WORDS[COMP_CWORD]}"

    if [ $COMP_CWORD -ge 1 ]; then
        COMPREPLY=( $( compgen -W '-t= --test= -h --help -h --help -v --version ' -- $cur) )
    fi
}

complete -o bashdefault -o default -o filenames -F _docopttestrb docopt-test.rb
```

It basically only autocompletes with `-t=`, which is an invalid option as far as docopt is concerned.

Is this a problem on my script or could this be a bug?

issue_title: Z3 sometimes outputs the result of objective functions before the satisfiability result
issue_body:
### Summary of the problem
When requesting that Z3 minimize or maximize an expression it sometimes outputs the result of the objective function *before* the satisfiability result. Given the following SMTLIB program:

```
(declare-const x Int)
(declare-const y Int)
(assert (< x 2))
(assert (> (- y x) 1))
(maximize (+ x y))
(check-sat)
```
### Expected behavior

```
sat
(objectives
 ((+ x y) oo)
)
```

### Actual behavior
```
$  z3 -in <<EOF
(declare-const x Int)
(declare-const y Int)
(assert (< x 2))
(assert (> (- y x) 1))
(maximize (+ x y))
(check-sat)
EOF

(+ x y) |-> oo
sat
```

### Other Information
Try the two programs out on rise4fun:
* http://rise4fun.com/Z3Opt/dp
* http://rise4fun.com/Z3Opt/wJZ

Note the extra newline. That changes the ordering of Z3's output.

issue_title: Compiling with "non-default" CUDA
issue_body:
Hi,

when I specify a custom CUDA path when building, eg. `-DCUDNN_ROOT=/usr/local/cuda-8.0/`, the compilation toolchain still use parts which comes from the default CUDA version installed at `/usr/local/cuda/` (on my cluster).

Thus, when compiling, I got the following error:
`nvcc fatal : Value 'sm_61' is not defined for option 'gpu-architecture'`
because CUDA 7.5 does not support this architecture (which is the CUDA version installed at `/usr/local/cuda/`)

To fix this, I had to modify the PATH env. variables:
`export PATH=usr/local/cuda-8.0/:$PATH`

It would be nice if this workaround was not necessary.

issue_title: Configure to build/release with CircleCI
Missing NGAM wrongly reported
issue_body:
**memote** is reporting missing NGAM when the lower bound of R_ATPM is zero.

I think it should check for the presence of NGAM but not force a particular value.

The principle of constraint-based modeling is that one iteratively refines a model by applying constraints and reducing the solution space as more data is acquired. The model is valid as long as the "true" phenotype is still somewhere inside the solution space. 

If NGAM is unknown because it was not yet calibrated from experimental data, a range of [0, max_flux] is still a valid constraint.

issue_title: Cloudformation parameter of type "number" expects basestring for value attributes
issue_body:
With the following code:

```
ASGMinSize = t.add_parameter(Parameter(
    "ASGMinSize",
    Default=1,
    MinValue=1,
    Type="Number",
    Description="Minimum Instances for Autoscaling Group.",
    MaxValue=2,
))
```

I am getting the error:

> TypeError: <class 'troposphere.Parameter'>: ASGMinSize.Default is <type 'int'>, expected <type 'basestring'>

This is easily fixed by quoting the fields such as:

```
ASGMinSize = t.add_parameter(Parameter(
    "ASGMinSize",
    Default="1",
    MinValue="1",
    Type="Number",
    Description="Minimum Instances for Autoscaling Group.",
    MaxValue="2",
))
```

As the former is how it was originally converted from cloudformation templates using /scripts/cfn2py and given a number field should be a numeric, it would make sense to allow a special type for the field `Default` and for `MinValue` and `MaxValue` to be of type integer.

issue_title: pex -c incorrectly alters exit codes
issue_body:
Running pytest from a pex always exits 0 if there are test failures, but not if there are command line argument processing errors.

For example, given this `test_fail.py`:

```python
assert False
```

A pytest pex will exit zero:

```console
$ pex --version && pex pytest -c py.test -o pytest.pex && ./pytest.pex test_fail.py ; echo $?
pex 1.0.1
=========================================================================== test session starts ============================================================================
platform darwin -- Python 2.7.10 -- py-1.4.30 -- pytest-2.7.2
rootdir: /Users/rouge8/forks/pex, inifile:
collected 0 items / 1 errors

================================================================================== ERRORS ==================================================================================
______________________________________________________________________ ERROR collecting test_fail.py _______________________________________________________________________
test_fail.py:1: in <module>
    assert False
E   assert False
========================================================================= 1 error in 0.00 seconds ==========================================================================
0
```

But running `py.test` in a virtualenv or similar will exit 1.

On the other hand, giving a bad option to `py.test` will exit non-zero, as expected:

```console
$ pex --version && pex pytest -c py.test -o pytest.pex && ./pytest.pex --bad-option test_fail.py ; echo $?
pex 1.0.1
usage: pytest.pex [options] [file_or_dir] [file_or_dir] [...]
pytest.pex: error: unrecognized arguments: --bad-option
2
```

issue_title: OPEN message graceful restart decoding issue
issue_body:
Debugging a issue in ExaBGP with graceful restart it seems that the Address family flag (which indicates that the routes for a address family was preserved) is not correctly decoded in the open message when a Graceful restart capable peer re-sends the open message.

After making sure that the neighbour sends the correct data and its not getting corrupted anywhere I managed to tracked down the issue to the method _unpack_capability_ under the file _graceful.py_ in the open capability folder of messages (i.e. exabgp/bgp/message/open/capability/graceful.py). [https://github.com/Exa-Networks/exabgp/blob/4.0/lib/exabgp/bgp/message/open/capability/graceful.py](https://github.com/Exa-Networks/exabgp/blob/4.0/lib/exabgp/bgp/message/open/capability/graceful.py)

Line 79 where the flag_family is being extracted from the message binary it seems that the address family offset is being decoded instead of the flag offset

Here is the block of code that I am refering to.
```python
while data:
			afi = AFI.unpack(data[:2])
			safi = SAFI.unpack(data[2])
			flag_family = ord(data[0])
```


The fix is very trivial and it just involves changing the flag_family to use data offset 3 (the next 8 bits after the previous 2 byte AFI and 1 byte SAFI).

```python
while data:
			afi = AFI.unpack(data[:2])
			safi = SAFI.unpack(data[2])
			flag_family = ord(data[3])
```

I am not quite sure if this was intentional however I can't see any reason to decode the address family instead of the flag when parsing the graceful restart capability. This issue seems to be present in both the master branch and the 4.0.

issue_title: md5 options behaves extemely wierd
Enabling group-updates causes exabgp to group multiple NLRI with multiple MP_(UN)REACH in one UPDATE message
issue_body:
<!---
Verify first that your issue/request is not already reported on GitHub.
Also test if the latest release, and master branch are affected too.
-->

##### ISSUE TYPE
<!--- Pick one below and delete the rest: -->
 - Bug Report


##### OS
<!---
Mention the OS you are running ExaBGP on (Linux variant if relevant)
-->
Linux
CentOS 6.8


##### VERSION
<!--- Paste verbatim the output from “exabgp --version” between quotes below -->
```
ExaBGP : 3.4.20
Python : 2.6.6 (r266:84292, Oct 31 2017, 08:19:29)  [GCC 4.4.7 20120313 (Red Hat 4.4.7-3)]
Uname  : #1 SMP Tue Oct 31 08:18:37 PDT 2017
```

##### ENVIRONMENT 
<!--- Paste verbatim the output from “exabgp --di” (let us know if the output is empty) between quotes below -->
```
empty
```

<!--- You can also use gist.github.com links for larger files -->

##### CONFIGURATION
<!--- Paste verbatim your configuration file -->
```
group template {
	group-updates;
	neighbor ::ffff:10.128.10.128 {
		local-address ::ffff:10.128.10.169;
		local-as 60000;
		peer-as 60000;
		family {
			ipv4 unicast;
			ipv4 flow;
			ipv6 unicast;
			ipv6 flow;
		}
		router-id 1.1.1.1;
		hold-time 90;
		capability {
			asn4;
			route-refresh;
		}
	}
	process announce-routes {
		run /usr/bin/python /usr/lib/python2.6/f5/flowspec/exabgp_pipe.py /var/flowspecd/exabgp.pipe.0 > /dev/null 2>&1;
	}
}

```

<!--- You can also use gist.github.com links for larger files -->

##### SUMMARY
ExaBGP groups multiple NLRIs (flow spec routes) in a single UPDATE message with 'group-updates' attribute enabled.

However, if the multiple flow spec routes have different next hops, this causes multiple MP_REACH (or MP_UNREACH) path attributes in the UPDATE message which gets rejected by the upstream router with following error message:

"UPDATE from A.B.C.D contains duplicate MP_REACH attribute"

Per RFC https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-ietf-idr-error-handling-19, section 5.1

----
An UPDATE message MUST NOT contain more than one of the following:
      non-empty Withdrawn Routes field, non-empty Network Layer
      Reachability Information field, MP_REACH_NLRI attribute, and
      MP_UNREACH_NLRI attribute.
----

##### STEPS TO REPRODUCE
With above configuration, send following routes:

exabgp: 868    processes     Command from process announce-routes : announce flow route { match { destination 11.11.12.0/24; } then { redirect 11.11.11.199; } } 
exabgp: 868    processes     Command from process announce-routes : announce flow route { match { destination 11.11.13.0/24; } then { redirect 11.11.11.198; } } 


##### EXPECTED RESULTS
Multiple flow spec NLRI with multiple MP_REACH attributes are not encoded in single BGP UPDATE message. Only multiple NLRI (flow spec) are encoded in single BGP UPDATE as long as they have same next hop (MP_REACH or MP_UNREACH) path attribute.

Note that I am not sure whether multiple NLRI itself are also allowed even if they have same MP_REACH in same UPDATE message.


##### ACTUAL RESULTS
Multiple flow spec NLRI with multiple MP_REACH attributes are encoded in single BGP UPDATE message (with group-updates enabled). This violates the RFC standard and breaks upstream router's handling of BGP UPDATE

##### IMPORTANCE
<!-- Please let us know if the issue is affecting you in a production environment -->

issue_title: `Response.from_futures` parameter `info_keys` is unnecessarily inconvenient and inconsistent
issue_body:
https://github.com/dwavesystems/dimod/blob/3b4243f81cf0c6d9c1e66dae5f9ea75d4a438d48/dimod/response.py#L442

Currently, the `info_keys` parameter requires you to know which which keys in the return value from your future will correspond to which keys in the `Response.info` dict. This is quite inconvenient if you are writing code that is intended to be agnostic to an underlying sampler, as you may not know what "format" the sampler will return for information about the samples. Furthermore, this parameter is inconsistent with `Response.from_dicts`, which simply accepts a `info` parameter.

Proposed solution: change `info_keys=None` to `info_key="info"`, such that when the future returns, dimod will simply take `future.result[info_key]` and stick it into the info of the newly created `Response`.

issue_title: DBN Bug: multiple CPDs for the same varaiable
issue_body:
I found this problem while working on #452 
```python
from pgmpy.models import DynamicBayesianNetwork as dbn
from pgmpy.factors import TabularCPD
import numpy as np
model = dbn()
model.add_edges_from([(('a',0),('b',1)),(('c',0),('b',1))])
values = np.split(np.random.rand(4),2)
a_cpd = TabularCPD(('b',1), 2, values, [('a',0)], 2)
c_cpd = TabularCPD(('b',1), 2, values, [('c',0)], 2)
model.add_cpds(a_cpd, c_cpd)
model.initialize_initial_state()
```
Here the 2 CPDs are for the same variable , when `self.check_model()` is done in [here](https://github.com/pgmpy/pgmpy/blob/dev/pgmpy/models/DynamicBayesianNetwork.py#L517)
This error will be raised

```python
if set(evidence) != set(parents if parents else []):
                    raise ValueError("CPD associated with {node} doesn't have "
                                     "proper parents associated with it.".format(node=node))` 
```
Because the number of parents will be more than the number of evidences.

If you confirmed that this is a problem, i think it can be solved simply by applying CPD product for all CPDs of the same variable.

issue_title: echo_site_tls example with Let's Encrypt: obsolete cipher suite

I am trying to run the [echo_site_tls example](https://github.com/crossbario/autobahn-python/tree/master/examples/twisted/websocket/echo_site_tls
) with a certificate from Let's Encrypt:

I replaced the line:
    `contextFactory = ssl.DefaultOpenSSLContextFactory('keys/server.key','keys/server.crt')`

with 
    `contextFactory = ssl.DefaultOpenSSLContextFactory('/etc/letsencrypt/live/mywebsite.com/privkey.pem','/etc/letsencrypt/live/mywebsite.com/file.pem')`

where `file.pem` is `cert.pem` or `fullchain.pem`

It does not work in any browser, with the latest version. 

On Chrome: 

`NET::ERR_CERT_AUTHORITY_INVALID`

The details are:
Your connection to mywebsite.com is encrypted using an obsolete cipher suite.

The connection uses TLS 1.2

The connection is encrypted and authenticated using AES_128_GCM and uses RSA as the key exchange mechanism.

issue_title: [Meta] Object Storage Configuration for RC1
issue_body:
This is a top level issue for improving object storage configuration in the `v2.0-RC1` milestone

- [x] Modify all components to use the [objectstorage.toml](https://github.com/deis/charts/blob/master/workflow-dev/tpl/objectstorage.toml)/[deis-objectstorage-secret.yaml](https://github.com/deis/charts/blob/master/workflow-dev/tpl/deis-objectstorage-secret.yaml) files for object storage configuration.
  - [x] [deis/builder](https://github.com/deis/builder): https://github.com/deis/builder/issues/224
  - [x] [deis/slugbuilder](https://github.com/deis/slugbuilder): https://github.com/deis/slugbuilder/issues/32
  - [x] [deis/slugrunner](https://github.com/deis/slugrunner): https://github.com/deis/slugrunner/issues/18
  - [x] [deis/postgres](https://github.com/deis/postgres): https://github.com/deis/postgres/issues/71
  - [x] [deis/charts](https://github.com/deis/charts): https://github.com/deis/charts/pull/171
  - [x] [deis/registry](https://github.com/deis/registry): https://github.com/deis/registry/pull/31
  - [x] [deis/controller](https://github.com/deis/controller): https://github.com/deis/controller/pull/535
  - [x] [deis/dockerbuilder](https://github.com/deis/dockerbuilder): https://github.com/deis/dockerbuilder/pull/41
- [x] Add the ability to specify the following 3 buckets in [objectstorage.toml](https://github.com/deis/charts/blob/master/workflow-dev/tpl/objectstorage.toml):
  - `database_bucket` - the bucket that the [database](https://github.com/deis/postgres) should back up to
  - change `bucket` to `slug_bucket` - this is the bucket that the [builder](https://github.com/deis/builder), [slugbuilder](https://github.com/deis/slugbuilder), and [slugrunner](https://github.com/deis/slugrunner) should use to pass tarballs and slugs between each other
  - `registry_bucket` - the bucket that the [registry](https://github.com/deis/registry) should save image layers to
- [x] Change [object storage documentation](https://github.com/deis/workflow/blob/master/src/installing-workflow/configuring-object-storage.md) to show how to configure object storage with the single `deis-object-storage-secret.yaml` based method (https://github.com/deis/workflow/issues/99)

issue_title: When can't find linux distribution name and version, none appears but is not clear why
issue_body: When a wagon is created and the platform tag is "linux_x86_64, the distro and version tags might be "none" since they were not retrieved by `platform.linux_distribution()` (like on arch). It should be stated in the docs.

issue_title: mrs.path.walk() skips incoming links with the same signature
issue_body: A sentence like *The big scary dog barks.* has more than one link going to bark with the same signature (e.g. `ARG1/EQ` here). The `mrs.path.walk()` function uses a dictionary for these links, so the first one gets overwritten by the second.

issue_title: Anaconda installs into my system root on OS X
issue_body:
I went through the Anaconda installer and I think because the user interface is unclear it has installed itself into my system root. This is non-standard behaviour.

When installers on OS X ask for a destination, picking your main Hard Drive is considered to be the choice of disk where to install something, not the literal path intended.

After installation `which python` returns `//anaconda/bin/python` which is also an incorrect path for OS X.

issue_title: duplicate log messages
issue_body:
Adding code

```python
class DbgViewHandler(logging.Handler):
    def emit(self, record):
        print("DbgViewHandler: %s" % record)

logger = logging.getLogger("logging_test")
logger.setLevel(logging.DEBUG)
logger.propagate = False
stream_handler = logging.StreamHandler()
stream_handler.setLevel(logging.WARNING)  # all going to stderr
dbgview = DbgViewHandler()  # all going to stdout
dbgview.setLevel(logging.DEBUG)
logger.addHandler(dbgview)
logger.addHandler(stream_handler)

logger.info("Hello there")
logger.warn("Whoa there")
```

To the bottom of conda/gateways/logging.py outputs too many copies of messages.

I think pass ext as 'ext_modules' to setup, and includes to setup as 'include_dirs'.

My aim was to use bam_aux_get to write more nogil/faster code, rather than interacting with the python objects, but I can't get it to work properly. Have you run into such an issue with linking?

issue_title: Keep header when file is closed
issue_body:
After closing an `AlignmenFile`, the `reference_name` attribute of `AlignedSegment` instances from that file cannot be accessed anymore, because the header data has been deleted. Example:

```python
from pysam import AlignmentFile

def load(path):
    with AlignmentFile(path) as bam:
        return next(bam)

al = load('some.bam')
al.reference_name
```

The last line results in the following error:

```
ValueError: I/O operation on closed file
```

Would it be possible to keep the header information around to support that operation after the file is closed? Would it be possible to store the reference names as Python strings in a tuple, and reference that in `AlignedSegment`? This way Python's own resource management mechanism would handle it.
