issue_title: archive old indexes with best_compression
issue_body:
Ideally we would optimize indexes with the "best_compression" codec. This uses less disk space (~53% of the space for apache log data, and should improve to ~43% in the future) but the tradeoff is that access to the _source and lucene stored fields are slower.

Maybe this can somehow be an optional part of the optimize process? Optimizing in this way should not be noticeably longer (my guestimate: ~ 5%-10% on average) as the cost of merging the stored fields from lz4 to deflate takes only 19% longer after https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/LUCENE-6115 and https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/LUCENE-6141.

See https://github.com/elasticsearch/elasticsearch/pull/8863 for more information. This only applies to elasticsearch 2.0 or higher.

issue_title: Add index type filter to show and delete indices command
issue_body:
I have a usecase where I need to delete a specific type of some indices instead of all the data.

This can be done by using the [delete_mapping](https://elasticsearch-py.readthedocs.org/en/master/api.html#elasticsearch.client.IndicesClient.delete_mapping) method.

IIUC, `curator.api.delete_indices` and `curator.api.delete` need to be extended with a `types` argument and `curator.cli.index_selection.indices` needs to have a new `@click.option` decorator.

If this is an OK way to implement, I can create a PR.

issue_title: Option to save (delete) snapshots by age-grouping
issue_body:
I have a cronjob taking snapshots of an ES cluster every 20 minutes.
This leads to a very large amount of snapshots, clogging up the repository if I don't clean up regularly.
Currently I'm just deleting everything older than 50 hours, but I'd also like to keep some older generations.

Example:
- Save 5 backups from the past 2 hours
- Save 1 backup per hour for time >2h && <12h
- Save 1 backup per day for time >12h && <7d
- Save 1 backup per week for time >7d && <30d
- Delete everything older than 1 month

Since it might be overly (and uselessly) complex to provide such a set of rules on the commandline, something like an exponential save function (a la exponential backoff) would suffice.
For instance, BackupPC has fairly straightforward machinery to support this: http://backuppc.sourceforge.net/faq/BackupPC.html#_conf_fullkeepcnt_

{"sort": [{"_score": {"order": "desc"}}], "from": 0, "fields": ["_id", "created"], "facets": {"services": {"_asdict": {"_asdict": {"_asdict": {"_asdict": {"_asdict": {"_asdict": {"_asdict": {"_asdict": {"_asdict": {"_asdict": {"_asdict": {"_asdict": {"_asdict": {"_asdict": {"_asdict": {"_asdict": {"_asdict": {"_asdict": {"_asdict": {"_asdict": {"_asdict": {"_asdict": {"_asdict": {"_asdict": {"_asdict": {"_asdict": {"_asdict": {"_asdict": {"_asdict": {"_asdict": {"_asdict": {"_asdict": {"_asdict": {"_asdict": {"_asdict": {"_asdict": {"_asdict" repeating forever
```
however
```
json.dumps(query_body)
{"sort": [{"_score": {"order": "desc"}}], "from": 0, "fields": ["_id", "created"], "facets": {"services": {"terms":  ...... "size": 20}
```
works just fine. If I remove the changes made by #94 everything works as expected in 1.4.0.

I also found this benchmark of json | simplejson | ultrajson https://medium.com/@jyotiska/json-vs-simplejson-vs-ujson-a115a63a9e26 that makes me question if defaulting to simplejson is the right thing to do.

Maybe when I upgrade all my code to use the es dsl library this won't be an issue anymore.

issue_title: Include a simple way of getting into a development environment
issue_body:
In my projects, I include a `devenv.sh` script that uses a docker container and a shared volume including the source directory that makes it incredibly painless for new users to make contributions.

If the `RUN` commands in the `Dockerfile` were externalized into a `./build.sh` script, then the `Dockerfile` could be used to the build the development container, and can still be used to do point in time builds of source code by running it with `docker build -t az-devenv .; docker run -v $(pwd):/az-cli -it az-devenv bash ./build.sh`.

That will allow the actual enlistment to override the source that was added with `ADD` in the `Dockerfile`.

Doing this would allow me to type `./devenv.sh` and get a fully working development environment, instead of spending a non-trivial amount of time trying to get Python and pythonpath and whatnot to be configured correctly.

I have a virtual network "vn1" in a resource group "rg-network".

issue_title: Commands are missing `--subscription-id`
issue_body:
This was a very important feature from `azure-xplat-cli` for me.

The fact that all commands took `--subscription-id` means that I can write scripts that have the user input their subscription ID and I can take action in that subscription without needing to change the global state.

Without this functionality, it's seems impossible to have concurrent running scripts that are running in different subscriptions.

issue_title: VirtualMachineSizeTypes mistmatch causes deserialization errors
issue_body:
Currently the VirtualMachineSizeTypes enum misses out the 'F' size types and include 'G' types which are not available in the UI.
https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-python/blob/master/azure-mgmt-compute/azure/mgmt/compute/models/compute_management_client_enums.py

Deserialization fails when we try to get the list of vms using
compute_client.virtual_machines.list_all() and we can't access the returned object.

It makes automation of vm creation impossible when a 'F' size is chosen.

issue_title: Cannot deploy file with UTF-8 BOM
issue_body:
The `_get_source` function does not handle encodings well, in particular, it breaks on a UTF-8 BOM even if the rest of the file is ASCII-compatible.

Would be better as:
```python
source = codecs.open(ourfile, 'r', 'utf-8-sig').read().encode('ascii', errors='strict')
```

But it would probably be better overall if the entire code generation used Unicode throughout (probably via a StringIO instance to avoid the concatenation performance penalties, though those aren't generally going to be a big deal here).

issue_title: Graphical mesh lag behind collision shape in debug draw.
issue_body:
Using `m_RigidBody->getMotionState()->getWorldTransform(trans);` causes the graphical mesh to lag behind the collision shape in DebugDraw call.
While `btTransform trans = m_RigidBody->getWorldTransform();` makes the collision shape and graphical mesh to align perfectly.

*note: **m_RigidBody** is an instance of **btRigidBody**.

Steps to reproduce:

1. Create 2 box shapes. One for ground and another a small box (mass = 10Kg)
2. Set ground box to (0, 0, 0) and smaller box at (0, 600, 0)
3. enable wireframe debug drawing.


Kindly find the images for my test on this page:
http://www.bulletphysics.org/Bullet/phpBB3/viewtopic.php?f=9&t=11689

issue_title: In btGeneric6DofConstraint the maximum motor force is used as an impulse despite the name
issue_body:
In btGeneric6DofConstraint one can set the maximum motor force with the btRotationalLimitMotor::m_maxMotorForce and the btTranslationalLimitMotor::m_maxMotorForce.
Despite the name this value will be used as an Impulse (and not as a Force).
This is confusing and also the user should know the deltatime to set a correct value and that is not always easy to obtain.

Checking other constraints:
In case of btHingeConstraint it is correctly called setMaxMotorImpulse.
In case of btSliderConstraint it is called setMaxLinMotorForce and it is correctly used as force.

I suggest to change the implementation similar to btSliderConstraint by simply dividing with the FPS.

(The problem exist also in btGeneric6DofSpringConstraint and btGeneric6DofSpring2Constraint)

issue_title: graceful shutdown or reload with gunicorn without shutdown connections
issue_body:
How can I do a graceful shutdown or reload with gunicorn when there are pending connections(will be finished a little later), ensure the pending connections be finished, not directly shutdown by aiohttp?

currently，if I run a reload to an aiohttp app with gunicorn，sometimes the client throws aiohttp.client_exceptions.ServerDisconnectedError。

look into the code，i found that if a client request connection created at the same time when reload，it will be shutdown。but my need is the connection shut after complete the http request&response。

```
# worker.py::GunicornWebWorker
    @asyncio.coroutine
    def close(self):
        if self.servers:
            servers = self.servers
            self.servers = None

            # stop accepting connections
            for server, handler in servers.items():
                self.log.info("Stopping server: %s, connections: %s",
                              self.pid, len(handler.connections))
                server.close()
                yield from server.wait_closed()

            # send on_shutdown event
            if hasattr(self.wsgi, 'shutdown'):
                yield from self.wsgi.shutdown()

            # stop alive connections
            tasks = [
                handler.shutdown(
                    timeout=self.cfg.graceful_timeout / 100 * 95)
                for handler in servers.values()]
            yield from asyncio.gather(*tasks, loop=self.loop)

            # cleanup application
            if hasattr(self.wsgi, 'cleanup'):
                yield from self.wsgi.cleanup()
```
when handler.connections > 0, i.e.,has pending connections, if the connection was just create, it will be shutdown throw:
    web_server.py::Server::shutdown -> web_protocol::RequestHandler::shutdown
    

```
# web_protocol::RequestHandler::shutdown
    @asyncio.coroutine
    def shutdown(self, timeout=15.0):
        """Worker process is about to exit, we need cleanup everything and
        stop accepting requests. It is especially important for keep-alive
        connections."""
        self._force_close = True
        # ...
```


here, by set self._force_close = True,
in data_received, the request will be ignored.


```
# web_protocol::RequestHandler::data_received
    @asyncio.coroutine
    def data_received(self, data):
        if self._force_close or self._close:
            return
        # ...
```


so, how to do a graceful shutdown or reload (fg, with gunicorn)?
a way of which is adding an on_shutdown handler, if one can check whether there are pending connections, he can wait until all connections finished with some seconds.
is this ok, or another better way?  thanks~~

issue_title: Refactor client api for sending requests
issue_body:
Current process is:
1. `ClientSession` creates request class
2. Acquires connection from connector
3. Sends request's headers via complicated procedure:
   
   3.1. `ClientRequest` sends headers
   
   3.2. It creates a task for sending request's BODY
   
   3.3.  After that request creates `ClientResponse` instance and returns it
   
   3.4. `ClientSession` starts response by `resp.start()` call. 
4. Outer code gets response object with preloaded headers and it's ready to process RESOPONSE BODY.

That looks overcomplicated. I believe `ClientSession` should send request and so on. Request should know nothing about Response.

Pros:
1. It makes the process for sending request and getting back response much more clear for my brain at least.
2. Later we can add _HTTP request pipelining_ for sending multiple requests without immediate waiting response for the same connection as browsers do. Historically `Connector` and `Connection` are second-class citizens but after introducing `ClientSession` we can bring up `ClientConnection`, e.g.

```
    async with session.connect(address) as conn:
        ask1 = conn.get('/data1')
        ask2 = conn.post('/data2', data=b'data')
        resp1 = yield from ask1
        data1 = yield from resp1.read()
        resp2 = yield from ask2
        data2 = yield from resp2.read()
```

Honestly I don't sure pipelining is so important, looks like python libraries just don't care.
But if we can support it -- why not?

Cons:
1. We will break current API. That part is not documented properly and I suspect nobody rely on. I believe we will not break any existing code but I should mention the breakage.
2. Requires significant changes in our code base but I'm volunteer for the task. 

@kxepal @fafhrd91 @popravich I need your opinion.

P.S. I want make a change to suppress warnings for unclosed response when session was closed properly. Looks like it requires significant changes for test suite anyway, that's why I'm asking right now. 
Another option is just adding yet another flag to `ClientResponse` or using weakrefs but I see the proper way.

issue_title: Allowing variable references in markdown cells
Using different favicon's would make navigation easier
issue_body:
Migrating from https://github.com/jupyter/jupyter/issues/118:

Since we are tending toward a full OS in the browser... ;-) I think it would help to be able to differentiate the jupyter-js-filebrowser, jupyter-js-editor, jupyter-js-terminal and jupyter-js-notebook navigation tabs from the favicon.

CC @dubourg

issue_title: Allowing variable references in Markdown cells
issue_body:
Having access to kernel runtime variables to insert into markdown cells (or other non-code cells/widgets) would be an excellent enhancement for notebooks, particularly when including detailed narrative that describes the results of the code and research. Before posting this, I looked around and found references that lead back to [earlier discussions around this on the ipython repo](https://github.com/ipython/ipython/issues/2958), and it seemed like there was interest in getting this implemented.

I searched and didn't see anything already covering this in the jupyter/notebook issue list, so I felt I should raise it here as well, in case key issues like this have not also been migrated along with the code to the new repo. Please see [the original issue](https://github.com/ipython/ipython/issues/2958) for a great discussion on this matter, as well as some ideas and implementation estimates.

Since Jupyter now is focused on a more language-neutral approach, I'm sure that complicates the design, but having this functionality, even starting with just Python-based notebooks, would be an attractive addition. I'd like to help move this forward in any way that I can.

And we can provide csv data in another cell by python.

issue_title: Revise  markdown"attachment" spec.
issue_body:
It seem we made a few unfortunate decision with the way we use attachments. 

 - It does not follow markdown spec: `![alt](/url "title")` imply that we :
    - need to use `attachment://` for the link to be an url
    - escape the filename as `)]` should **not** be present. 

Have to check if the attachment thing was released to know if we can fix it without backward compat. .

issue_title: Update MathJax to 2.6, to fix rendering artifact
issue_body:
I'm getting the following artifact of a right sided border on rendered MathJax objects (see image) from either manually created LaTeX code (as below) or Sympy expression renderings. The issue happens for both $ ... $ and $$ ... $$ items, where the MathJax object terminates; inline expressions will have them, too. This was working on the default install of a plain Python installation, using notebook 4.1, but under the most recent distribution of Anaconda, it's happening.

![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/197778/12616595/0625ccb8-c4da-11e5-9c58-3f1e90dabf36.png)

```
We know the following:
* Side AC = 500
* Side BC = 300
* Angle BAC = 36.87
* Angle ABC = 90

We need to solve for side AB. As described, angle BAC allows us to use its cosine ratio to derive AB, since it is adjacent and side AC is opposite angle ABC, and thus is the hypotenuse:

$\cos{36.87} = \frac{AB}{500}$  
$500 \cdot \cos{36.87} = AB$  
$400 = AB$

We can check this using the Pythagorean theorem, $a^2 + b^2 = c^2$:  
$300^2+b^2=500^2$  
$90000+b^2=250000$  
$b^2 = 160000$  
$b = \sqrt{160000} = 400$
```

I can do further testing, but wanted to at least bring this to your attention first to know if I'm missing something here. Thanks!

issue_title: 2.0.0 Defaults, not copied but using reference

model = MyModel()
```

rather than 

```
model = MyModel({})
```

I end up using defaults as reference and not copy. Ending with all subsequent objects to inherit their sibling value as default.

```
import unittest

from schematics import Model
from schematics.types import ListType, StringType

class MyModel(Model):
    items = ListType(StringType, default=[])


class TestIterable(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_without_value(self):
        model = MyModel()
        model.items.append('foo')
        model.items.append('bar')

        self.assertEqual(len(model.items), 2)

        model2 = MyModel()
        self.assertEqual(len(model2.items), 0)  # This won't work. DUH. 

    def test_with_value(self):
        model = MyModel({})
        model.items.append('foo')
        model.items.append('bar')

        self.assertEqual(len(model.items), 2)

        model2 = MyModel({})
        self.assertEqual(len(model2.items), 0)
```

issue_title: The FileStores should belong to the Broker, not the EventSources
Eliminate config options for approximate input file size
issue_body:
Asking the user to specify the input size is error prone and inconvenient.

Whenever we download a file to a local disk for the purpose of uploading it to the job store, we should switch to using Toil's import functionality instead. It uses streaming instead of local disk thereby eliminating the need for estimating a disk requirement for the import job. As imports are implemented in Toil right now, this approach might be less reliable and slower than using s3am but we can address those issues in Toil if and when they occur.

What do we do in cases where files are processed immediately after being downloaded from an external location and the job store upload is skipped? Not skipping is one option. Trying to determine the file size is another. For HTTP this can be done with a HEAD request, for S3 there is a API call, probably also being a HEAD request under the hood.

issue_title: CentOS71 docker to use LLVM3.9
issue_body:
Thought I should share that I am able to build CoreCLR (and CoreRT native code) with the following dockerfile:

```dockerfile
FROM centos:7
# it should pick the latest in 7 (atm 7.3.1611)
RUN cat /etc/*-release

RUN yum -y -q  install epel-release
RUN yum-config-manager --add-repo https://copr.fedorainfracloud.org/coprs/alonid/llvm-3.9.1/repo/epel-7/alonid-llvm-3.9.1-epel-7.repo 
RUN yum -y -q update
RUN yum -y -q install \
      git cmake make icu libicu-devel lttng-ust lttng-ust-devel libuuid-devel \
      libunwind libunwind-devel llvm-3.9.1 clang-3.9.1 lldb-3.9.1 \
      lldb-3.9.1-devel --enablerepo=alonid-llvm-3.9.1

ENV PATH /opt/llvm-3.9.1/include:/opt/llvm-3.9.1/lib64:/opt/llvm-3.9.1/bin:$PATH

RUN git clone https://github.com/dotnet/coreclr -b master --single-branch \
    && cd coreclr \
    && ./build.sh
```

These are the minimum set of dependencies required to build the said repos.

However, the one [in the repo](https://github.com/dotnet/core/blob/d5ae4db/tools/dotnet-bootstrap/base/lab/containers/centos71/Dockerfile) seems to be using LLVM, Clang and LLDB version 3.6 and bit of extra dependencies.

issue_title: Certificate signed but not installed
issue_body:
I followed the instruction from the BETA email as follows:

    git clone https://github.com/letsencrypt/letsencrypt
    cd letsencrypt
    ./letsencrypt-auto --agree-dev-preview --server \
       https://acme-v01.api.letsencrypt.org/directory auth
and I fill out the desired hostname etc.

I receive this response:
    
    IMPORTANT NOTES:
    - Congratulations! Your certificate and chain have been saved at
    /etc/letsencrypt/live/iontrap.phys.au.dk/fullchain.pem. Your cert
    will expire on 2016-02-02. To obtain a new version of the
    certificate in the future, simply run Let's Encrypt again.

Indicating that Let's encrypt was succesful, but no indication of what further action is needed on my part. I was using a self-signed certificate which is still the one being used by apache2.

1) It was my understanding that running the letsencrypt should complete the install. Perhaps a note at the beginning should indicate what is the expected outcome of running the program e.g.:

"This is Let's encrypt. By running this command you will generate, have signed, and install a certificate to your webserver"

2) Then if letsencrypt does not in the end install the certificate it could have an (additional) important note saying:

"Letsencrypt did not install the newly signed certificate since you are already using a certificate.
To replace this certificate run this command (or see this website) or change this config-file"
or it could ask whether to install the certificate.

I believe that otherwise users would try to manually point apache2 to the new certs manually or perhaps copy the new certs to the location of the old certs which would then not work when the certs where to be renewed.

issue_title: tzname() inconsistent between tzwin() and tzwinlocal() and neither reflect actual DST.
issue_body:
Time zone names are inconsistent between `tzwin()` and `tzwinlocal()` and neither reflect actual DST.

Tested on Windows for all US time zones with current master SHA1: b46e721 and for proposed fix at #142 - both fail.

```python
import datetime
import dateutil.tz

tw = dateutil.tz.tzwin('Eastern Standard Time')
twl = dateutil.tz.tzwinlocal()
tests = [('EDT should be started', '2015-03-08 03:01'),
         ('Still not EDT', '2015-03-15 01:59'),
         ('tzwin is EDT', '2015-03-15 02:00'),
         ('tzwinlocal still not EDT', '2015-03-16 23:59'),
         ('Finally EDT!', '2015-03-17 00:00'),
         ('EDT should have ended', '2015-11-01 03:01'),
         ('Still not EST', '2015-11-08 01:59'),
         ('tzwin is EST', '2015-11-08 02:00'),
         ('tzwinlocal still not EST', '2015-11-15 23:59'),
         ('Finally EST!', '2015-11-16 00:00')]

row = '{:<16}  |  {:<21}  |  {:<21}  |  {}'
print row.format('Date', 'tzwin', 'tzwinlocal', 'Notes')
print '{:-<100}'.format('')
for each in tests:
    desc, date = each
    test = datetime.datetime.strptime(date, '%Y-%m-%d %H:%M')
    tw_test = test.replace(tzinfo=tw)
    twl_test = test.replace(tzinfo=twl)
    print row.format(date, tw_test.tzname(), twl_test.tzname(), desc)
```
result:
```
Date              |  tzwin                  |  tzwinlocal             |  Notes
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
2015-03-08 03:01  |  Eastern Standard Time  |  Eastern Standard Time  |  EDT should be started
2015-03-15 01:59  |  Eastern Standard Time  |  Eastern Standard Time  |  Still not EDT
2015-03-15 02:00  |  Eastern Daylight Time  |  Eastern Standard Time  |  tzwin is EDT
2015-03-16 23:59  |  Eastern Daylight Time  |  Eastern Standard Time  |  tzwinlocal still not EDT

issue_title: Trace calls to key classes
Use value for "missing" in schema as "default" in Swagger spec
issue_body:
When the schema represents a response, it makes sense that the "default" keyword for a field represents the "default" value in the Swagger spec.  However, when using the schema as a request body parser, it's actually the "missing" value that becomes the "default" for deserialization of the request body. Ideally this would be part of the Swagger specification.

This is somewhat related to #127

issue_title: Using Python built-in Enum type in sqlalchemy.Enum column type produces wrong oneOf validation
issue_body:
``` python
class Choices(enum.Enum):
    a = 'a'
    b = 'b'
    c = 'c'

class MyModel(db.Model):
    # ...
    selected_choice = db.Column(db.Enum(Choices), nullable=False)

class MyModelSchema(ModelSchema):
    class Meta:
        model = MyModel
        fields = ['selected_choice']
```

The `MyModelSchema` end up having

```
selected_choice.validate[0].choices == (<enum 'ChoicesEnum'>,)
```

(notice a tuple), because `MyModel.selected_choice.type.enums` stores a tuple of an enum for some reason...

BTW, constructing a marshmallow field with `oneOf(ChoicesEnum)` (omit the unnecessary tuple wrapping) doesn't help much:

``` python
>>> f = fields.Str(validate=[
            validate.OneOf(ChoicesEnum),
         ])
>>> f.serialize('a', ChoicesEnum.a)
'ChoicesEnum.a'
```

And I couldn't make it to deserialize.

A related discussion that I have found: https://github.com/marshmallow-code/marshmallow-sqlalchemy/pull/2

issue_title: Option for POST to update existing matching resource
issue_body:
It's often useful to have an idempotent version of POST that will update an existing object if certain fields match, rather than always creating a new one. This makes it easy for clients to POST again and still get the same result, e.g. if something went wrong the first time. 

Maybe the bundle could be hydrated first to populate all the fields and resolve any related fields, then it's just a case of replacing the bundle.obj with any that matches specific fields (either all fields provided in the request, or a sub-set defined for the resource).

Could be done with Meta properties:

    class Meta:
        post_will_update = True
        post_match_fields = ['name', 'date']

Or a method to replace bundle.obj with any existing resource that matches the hydrated-but-not-saved bundle.obj:

    def obj_matching(self, bundle):
        try:
            # Replace with existing object with same name and date.
            bundle.obj = MyModel.objects.get(name=bundle.obj.name, date=bundle.obj.date)
        except MyModel.DoesNotExist:
            pass
        return bundle

issue_title: Integrate DLModel with Spark ML's ImageSchema.readImages()
issue_body:
As of [a few months ago](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/SPARK-21866), it looks like now Spark has [native support](https://blogs.technet.microsoft.com/machinelearning/2018/03/05/image-data-support-in-apache-spark/) for reading directories of images into a Spark Dataframe. Specifically, the `pyspark.ml.image.ImageSchema.readImages(path_to_img_folder)` function returns a DataFrame that has the schema:
```
root
 |-- image: struct (nullable = true)
 |    |-- origin: string (nullable = true)
 |    |-- height: integer (nullable = false)
 |    |-- width: integer (nullable = false)
 |    |-- nChannels: integer (nullable = false)
 |    |-- mode: integer (nullable = false)
 |    |-- data: binary (nullable = false)
```

Now that this image DataFrame functionality exists, has anyone tried to create a Spark ML Pipeline that starts with `ImageSchema.readImages()`, converts the image binary data to `Arrays` (or `mlllib` `Vector/Matrix`), and then uses a BigDL model to perform image featurization using a pre-trained `DLModel`? 

My goal is to implement a Spark Pipeline like: `Image URIs --> ImageSchema.readImages(), get a DataFrame with binary image data --> Reshape image data to Multi-dimensional arrays --> Transform DataFrame using pre-trained DLModel (e.g. ResNet50 featurizer) --> Ouptut n-dim Feature Vectors`.

This [house similarities](https://software.intel.com/en-us/articles/using-bigdl-to-build-image-similarity-based-house-recommendations) article is the closest snippet I could find to a code sample where a `DLClassifier` is applied to a DataFrame with image data (in the form of `Arrays`), but I haven't yet found any way to integrate this with Spark's new `ImageSchema` functionality.

Anyone have recommendations on what the best way to proceed with such an image featurization pipeline would be, or have links to any code samples that accomplish something like this? I'd like to keep as much computation within the same Spark ML pipeline as possible.

Thanks in advance!

issue_title: Integer check in extract does not allow long type
issue_body:
In Python 2.x, the following check does not allow `low` or `high` to be of type `long`:

```_z3_assert(isinstance(high, int) and high >= 0 and isinstance(low, int) and low >= 0, "First and second arguments must be non negative integers")```

You can fix this with `isinstance(high, (int, long))`, for example.

issue_title: Setting :random-seed has practically no effect, Renaming functions has huge effect
issue_body:
I am trying to evaluate the performance of different SMT2 encoding schemes. To this goal I create test cases using the encodings I'm trying to compare and run each test case many times with different `:random-seed` settings. However, it turns out that setting `:random-seed` has minimal to no effect. Randomly renaming the functions I declare/define without changing anything else on the other hand changes the performance of my test cases by more than 2x!

See [this SO question for more information](http://stackoverflow.com/questions/42371139/how-to-analyse-z3-performance-issues). The test cases `test1.smt2`, `test2.smt2`, and `test3.smt2` used in the analysis below can be found [here](http://svn.clifford.at/handicraft/2017/z3perf/).

On my workstation, and with latest z3 (c67cf16), the test cases take about 2 minutes to execute.

I ran each of them eight times with `:random-seed` set to 1 .. 8. The runtime for each of the test cases is for the most part not effected by the `:random-seed` setting:

![results](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/619764/23220462/5dda6126-f922-11e6-8d5f-a0a04ab3c2fc.png)

As mentioned above: **The three test cases are practically identical! The only difference is the names I use for the identifiers I declare/define.**

I am now considering writing a preprocessor that randomly renames the functions in an smt2 file so I can use it in addition to setting `:random-seed`..

There is an argument to be had that a solver should preserve the order in which statements are processed, because quite often related clauses show up in the SMT2 file close to each other. But I have never heard such an argument with regard to the identifiers declared/defined in the SMT2 input. I think whatever random changes I inject into the solver by renaming my functions, the same kind of random changes should be triggered by changing the value of `:random-seed`.

issue_title: send_mavlink() doesn't autofix system id
issue_body:
When sending mavlink messages created with message_factory, the correct system id must be specified, otherwise it won't work.

This was tested using the following code:

```python
 msg = vehicle.message_factory.mount_control_encode(
        0, 0,  # target system, target component
        30 * 100,  # pitch
        0,  # roll
        0,  # yaw
        0)  # save trim
```

However, changing the system id to the proper value (1 in this case) fixes the problem.
The documentation suggests that this should be automatically taken care of by send_mavlink(), yet it does't seem to be the case.

I tried the same code with dronekit 1 and it does indeed work without the need to specify the sysid manually.

issue_title: Documentation: python modules
issue_body:
Subsequent to a discussion over at https://github.com/Dronecode/MAVProxy/issues/181.

The dronekit documentation should make it clear that, if the user uses the Windows installer, MAVProxy (and dronekit) will not have visibility of the user's default Python environment. Any extra Python modules they wish to use should be copied into the MAVProxy folder.

issue_title: 关于StockCommission中的get_commission的疑问
issue_body:
rqalpha在StockCommission类的get_commission方法中计算股票手续费, 其代码如下:

```
        commission = self.commission_map[order_id]
        cost_money = trade.last_price * trade.last_quantity * self.rate * self.multiplier
        if cost_money > commission:
            if commission == self.min_commission:
                self.commission_map[order_id] = 0
                return cost_money
            else:
                self.commission_map[order_id] = 0
                return cost_money - commission
        else:
            if commission == self.min_commission:
                self.commission_map[order_id] -= cost_money
                return commission
            else:
                self.commission_map[order_id] -= cost_money
                return 0
```

上述代码中使用了一个self.commission_map, 是考虑到一个Order可能会通过多次交易成交完成, 但导致如下问题:
1. 代码啰嗦, 难以理解.
2. 与现实不太符合。
    现实中的手续费是与订单关联的, 最小手续费是与订单相关的, 与该订单是通过多少次交易完成无关. 
3. 由于多了一个self.commission_map, 在运行过程中self.commission_map是随订单数不断增大的, 未释放, 浪费内存.

建议将计算手续费功能与Order关联,  而不是与Trade关联, 与现实保持一致, 不但去掉了一个commission_map, 而且代码会简单的多,如下:
```
        cost_money = order._avg_price * order._filled_quantity * self.rate
        if cost_money > self.min_commission:
            return cost_money
        else:
            return self.min_commission
```

issue_title: Enhancement request: Join/Leave Event on Users
issue_body:
Hi @be-hase / @GuitarSucks 

Currently, Join/Leave events are based on **self**. In other words, when Bot itself joins/leaves a group/room, it will receive the Join/Leave event.

The functionality my application is looking for, is Join/Leave events based on **users**. This means when a new user joins a group/room (in which Bot is present), Bot gets an event (preferably, this event includes userID of new user). This way, Bot can issue welcome message to the group/room, and give introductory instructions to new joins.

Will you consider this enhancement request? Thank you.

issue_title: enhancement: axis argument to transforms
issue_body:
At present, transforms can only be performed on arrays of the corresponding dimensionality. It would be useful to introduce an `axis` argument to allow the transform to be computed over arbitrary array dimensions, e.g.

```python
a = np.ones((3, 4))
pywt.dwt(a, wavelet='db3', axis=-1)
```

This can be trivially implemented for `dwt` by using `pywt._pywt.dwt_axis` (introduced in #104), but will require some thought for higher dimensions.

The API would be the same as that for `np.fft.fft2` and `np.fft.fftn`:

```markdown
Signature: np.fft.fft2(a, s=None, axes=(-2, -1))
Docstring:
Compute the 2-dimensional discrete Fourier Transform

This function computes the *n*-dimensional discrete Fourier Transform
over any axes in an *M*-dimensional array by means of the
Fast Fourier Transform (FFT).  By default, the transform is computed over
the last two axes of the input array, i.e., a 2-dimensional FFT.

Parameters
----------
a : array_like
    Input array, can be complex
s : sequence of ints, optional
    Shape (length of each transformed axis) of the output
    (`s[0]` refers to axis 0, `s[1]` to axis 1, etc.).
    This corresponds to `n` for `fft(x, n)`.
    Along each axis, if the given shape is smaller than that of the input,
    the input is cropped.  If it is larger, the input is padded with zeros.
    if `s` is not given, the shape of the input along the axes specified
    by `axes` is used.
axes : sequence of ints, optional
    Axes over which to compute the FFT.  If not given, the last two
    axes are used.  A repeated index in `axes` means the transform over
    that axis is performed multiple times.  A one-element sequence means
    that a one-dimensional FFT is performed.
```


```markdown
Signature: np.fft.fftn(a, s=None, axes=None)
Docstring:
Compute the N-dimensional discrete Fourier Transform.

This function computes the *N*-dimensional discrete Fourier Transform over
any number of axes in an *M*-dimensional array by means of the Fast Fourier
Transform (FFT).

Parameters
----------
a : array_like
    Input array, can be complex.
s : sequence of ints, optional
    Shape (length of each transformed axis) of the output
    (`s[0]` refers to axis 0, `s[1]` to axis 1, etc.).
    This corresponds to `n` for `fft(x, n)`.
    Along any axis, if the given shape is smaller than that of the input,
    the input is cropped.  If it is larger, the input is padded with zeros.
    if `s` is not given, the shape of the input along the axes specified
    by `axes` is used.
axes : sequence of ints, optional
    Axes over which to compute the FFT.  If not given, the last ``len(s)``
    axes are used, or all axes if `s` is also not specified.
    Repeated indices in `axes` means that the transform over that axis is
    performed multiple times.
```

I don't know how useful this would be but it seems a natural step in extending the API. I'm not sure what exactly you'd do with the higher dimensional stuff but I have used a one-dimensional (continuous) wavelet transform over 3D data before.

issue_title: Scope of this project and possible future requirements
issue_body:
Hey everyone,

I'm working on another library specifically for discovering and controlling uPnP devices and realise that your implementation of SSDP discovery is far superior (great job!). I believe I'm right in thinking though that actual control of uPnP devices is beyond the scope of this library, otherwise I'd submit patches here.

My question is whether to include this whole library as a dependency (just to use the `ssdp` module), or to lift the `ssdp` module (copyright notice, attribution and all) and just use that. My worry with including this as a dependency is that there's potential for many more network discovery protocols to be implemented in `netdisco` which could depend on even more libraries I wouldn't need.

I recognise that this isn't your problem, I just wonder what your thoughts would be on the best way for me to proceed here?

Thanks for your time!
Ellis

issue_title: Search for pytube
issue_body:
I will be modeling search off of the search I created for vets.gov.  Since search for video can be complex I will take the following approach, in stages:

##Stage 0 - setting up search - Due August 4th

In this stage I will translate i14y to python, found here: https://github.com/GSA/i14y and set it up on AWS so that we can use elastic search as our search backend.

##Stage 1 - Naive search - due August 5th

In this version of search I will search based on known text - specifically search will be by title as well as by the person giving the talk

##Stage 2 - Labeled search added - due August 15th

In this version of search  I will search based on known text, and hand labeling that I will add to each video.

##Stage 3 - Classification for labeled search - due August 29th

In this version of search I will write scripts that classify videos into high level labels based on video title and presenter

##Stage 4 - Audio to text - due September 30th

In this version of search I will translate the video audio into text, allowing me to pull out summaries of the videos to appear below the videos as well as adding them to our search.  

##Stage 5 - Adding topic models allowing for classification by topic due October 30th

In this version I will add topic modeling to the audio of the video so that users can search by topic instead of by user or keyword alone.

##Stage 6 - Improving Search

Here I will begin adding tests to semantically check that search works intuitively for users.  Here will be more of an exploratory phase - looking into various summarization methods as well as other ways of efficiently doing search.  The i14y code base will be revisited and further changes will be made to ensure that search runs well.

issue_title: Unexpected output in template

I am upgrading a (closed-source) project to Aspen 1.0rc2. In one simplate I conditionally assign a variable `output` in the request-time Python section, and then conditionally show a message based on the truthiness of the variable. Under 1.0rc2, there is an `output` object in the default simplate context, so the value is always truthy. I know we've touched on this area before, but I forget where thing stand: am I supposed to know this somehow and just be careful with a name like that?

I think the only ever place we really hit this was with `response`, because we'd do an HTTP call from inside a simplate and bind the result to `response`, which would overwrite the in-progress *Aspen* response. Should we rename this to `aspen_output` to avoid the conflict? Should we prefix other names, like `path` and `qs`?

if foo:

issue_title: ACMEv2: Use newNonce for nonce?
issue_body:
If acme doesn't have a nonce stored, it does a HEAD to the same resource it's about to POST to, to get a nonce. This works as long as the server responds with a nonce on that resource, but it is not required to according to the spec. A HEAD will (often) return an error, and the spec says:

> ...and SHOULD provide it in error responses as well.

Should the acme client relay on a SHOULD? According to the spec, clients should use the newNonce url to get fresh nonces.

issue_title: cupy arrays are not fully compatible with thinc, hindering GPU support
issue_body:
Branching off of my comments on spacy bug [spacy#1798](https://github.com/explosion/spaCy/issues/1798#issuecomment-416026767), it would appear that cupy arrays, used in the course of using the GPU, are different enough from numpy arrays as to prevent several portions of thinc from working. Case in point:
```python
def begin_update(self, keys_values_lengths, drop=0.):
        cdef uint64_t[::1] keys <-
        cdef long[::1] lengths
        cdef float[::1] values
        keys, values_, lengths = keys_values_lengths <-
```
fails because cupy arrays do not support the implicit conversion that I think has to happen here for this to work. When I force the function that calls that to pass a numpy array, it works but it reveals a deadly mixing of numpy and cupy arrays around thinc. How does one get GPU support without cupy causing these problems?

issue_title: Remote execution of workflows / auxiliary steps.
issue_body:
It is even conceptually unclear what sos should do for the execution of workflows remotely. The problem is that if we call the workflow with `sos_run`, it would trigger other steps, which might or might not use remote targets. Also, it is unclear how to trigger auxiliary step from a remote step ...

The bottom line is that our current remote execution model is limited to single tasks, not workflows.

issue_title: "Reporters" or "converters" for more intelligent report generation
issue_body:
Currently the `report` action depends on users to perform all conversion and formatting of reported items. For example, if we need to report an image, or table, the user needs to know the exact markdown code to embed image or table, and a different action would be written if we are reporting using a different format, even a different markdown-processing engine with different md syntax. Note that image can have different formats and table can be a text file, an excel file, or a variable.

We could potentially provide some *reporter* mechanism to automate this process. For example, a reporter would convert excel file to markdown table, and another reporter would convert it to html table. Even if we cannot implement all of them, we can leave room for plugins.

issue_title: Low classification accuracy of Resnets?
issue_body:
I am trying to use the pretrained resnet models but the classification accuracy is < 10%. The same preprocessing works fine with the pretrained alexnet (Top-1 > 50%). Here is my script (the target label is part of the image name in my configuration, all images are taken from the Imagenet validation set):

```
import torchvision.models as models
from foolbox.models import PyTorchModel
import tqdm

net = models.resnet152(pretrained=True).cuda()

def preprocess_fn(x):
    x = x / 255
    x -= np.array([0.485, 0.456, 0.406])[None,:,None,None]
    x /= np.array([0.229, 0.224, 0.225])[None,:,None,None]
    return x.copy()

import glob
images = glob.glob("images/imagenet/*.png")

top1 = 0

for image_path in tqdm.tqdm(images):
    image = Image.open(image_path)
    image_size = 300
    image = image.resize((image_size, image_size), Image.ANTIALIAS)
    image = np.array(image).astype(np.float32)
    image = np.transpose(image, [2,0,1])
    label = int(image_path.split('.')[0].split('_')[1])
    
    image = torch.from_numpy(preprocess_fn(image[None])).cuda()
    image = Variable(image, volatile=True)
    q = net(image).data.cpu().numpy()[0]
    
    top1 += np.argmax(q) == label
    
print(top1 / len(images))
```

issue_title: Support for bootstrap4-alpha5 left column labels for checkboxes.
issue_body:
Bootstrap4 now supports labels for checkbox (#658), which I find very useful thing. For example you can have label "Agreement" and near checkbox write add another label which is more explaining. In such way form design/layout isn't getting broken with gaps.

![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/445304/20827879/3836f0a0-b87d-11e6-83a3-5965d4738d6a.png)

In terms of consistency I think current label should go into left label column, then all fields would have consistency and no exceptions and label near checkbox input should be optional. By doing this we could also get rid of bunch of exceptions in field templates and managing offset for central column as well.

The questions are:
1. Do you agree, that main label go into left column?
2. If yes, then which variable we should use for label near checkbox?
3. Maybe other suggestions?

First idea that came to my mind is - we could use `help_text` as label near checkbox field. Although this would make an exception and help text for checkboxes would have different look. 
On the other hand do someone really need label near input AND help text right below?

issue_title: [V3 CustomCom] Add internationalization to strings and generate messages.pot
[Core V3] Should we add cog paths to sys path?
issue_body:
## The Problem
Everytime you use `!load cogname` internally we have to turn that name into `cogs.cogname` before we can ship it to d.py and get it loaded. That means that we have `"cogs." +` sprinkled throughout the core bot code and it makes everything quite fragile and hard to improve/extend.

## My conceptual solution
Add cog path(s) to sys path. This will allow us to use `importlib` to systematically find cog modules wherever they may be. It will also allow us to have *multiple* cog paths. For you cog developers out there this means that you can add your repo folder to the list of known cog paths and you will not have to copy/paste your cog file into the bot folder in order to test anymore.

## The problem with the solution
If the bot owner has installed a python package with the same name as your cog, your cog will NOT be found and loaded (probably, we might be able to pull some magic to make this happen but I'm not sure on that yet).

## What are your thoughts?

issue_title: Add example for using batches if you want to accessing all the genome
issue_body:
I'm using the library to iterate over the entire genome. It's *way* faster to do this in batches, rather than calling `.compute()` on each variant in turn. I.e. I now do

`bgen['genotype'][x:(x+batch_size),:,0:2].compute()`

rather than 

```
for i in range(x, x+batch_size):
    bgen['genotype'][i,:,0:2].compute()
```

In fact, the `.compute()` call seems takes almost the same time regardless of whether it is getting 1 or 100 variants. It's obvious in hindsight, but it's probably worth saying that in the documentation, or even giving an example. Of course, if the batch size is too high, you run out of memory, so you would have to recommend tweaking this for the machine you are running on. But I had at least a 100x speedup in my code by batching the `.compute()` requests in this way.

issue_title: Simulating an FMU with no outputs produced by OpenModelica 0.13 nightly results in  'Bad file descriptor' exception in Ubuntu 16.04 with Python 2.7
Multiplicity in EParameter
issue_body:
Hi, 

I'm currently using the ``EParameter`` class of the ``pyecore`` library. I saw that this is a derived class of the ``ETypedElement`` class in which one has the option to determine a value for the upper/lower index. 
As far as I've seen, this is not possible in the definition of an ``EParameter`` directly, e.g. like

``` Python
p = Ecore.EParameter('p', eType= Ecore.EInt, required= True, lower = 0, upper = 5)
```
Are there any other possiblities to specify the multiplicity directly or would it be better to use ``Ecore.EList`` as the parameter type?
  
Thank you in advance!

issue_title: time_stats forms inappropriate URI
issue_body:
## Description of the problem, including code/CLI snippet

I have used the api to grab a list of issues and iterate through them getting their time stats:

    def display_issues(self, issues: Iterator[ProjectIssue]):
        rows = []
        for issue in issues:
            row = {k: getattr(issue, k) for k in ['iid', 'title', 'state', 'closed_at']}
            row['closed_at'] = pd.to_datetime(row['closed_at'], utc=True).strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M") \
                if row['closed_at'] else ""
            ts = issue.time_stats()
            row['estimate'] = ts['human_time_estimate']
            row['spent'] = ts['human_total_time_spent']
            row['assignee'] = issue.assignee['username'] if issue.assignee else ""
            rows.append(row)

It takes a very long time.  I discovered when running in debug mode that each call to time_stats() is failing once on an inappropriate formed URI.  Here is the debugging output

DEBUG:root:Stage: Display Issues
DEBUG:urllib3.connectionpool:https://gitlab.com:443 "GET /api/v4/projects/5118242/issues//237/time_stats HTTP/1.1" 308 0
DEBUG:urllib3.connectionpool:Resetting dropped connection: gitlab.com
DEBUG:urllib3.connectionpool:https://gitlab.com:443 "GET /api/v4/projects/5118242/issues/237/time_stats HTTP/1.1" 200 105
DEBUG:urllib3.connectionpool:https://gitlab.com:443 "GET /api/v4/projects/5118242/issues//235/time_stats HTTP/1.1" 308 0
DEBUG:urllib3.connectionpool:Resetting dropped connection: gitlab.com
DEBUG:urllib3.connectionpool:https://gitlab.com:443 "GET /api/v4/projects/5118242/issues/235/time_stats HTTP/1.1" 200 105
DEBUG:urllib3.connectionpool:https://gitlab.com:443 "GET /api/v4/projects/5118242/issues//227/time_stats HTTP/1.1" 308 0
DEBUG:urllib3.connectionpool:Resetting dropped connection: gitlab.com
DEBUG:urllib3.connectionpool:https://gitlab.com:443 "GET /api/v4/projects/5118242/issues/227/time_stats HTTP/1.1" 200 97
DEBUG:urllib3.connectionpool:https://gitlab.com:443 "GET /api/v4/projects/5118242/issues//226/time_stats HTTP/1.1" 308 0
DEBUG:urllib3.connectionpool:Resetting dropped connection: gitlab.com
....

I have found the error is in this code:

class TimeTrackingMixin(object):
    @cli.register_custom_action(('ProjectIssue', 'ProjectMergeRequest'))
    @exc.on_http_error(exc.GitlabTimeTrackingError)
    def time_stats(self, **kwargs):
        """Get time stats for the object.

        Args:
            **kwargs: Extra options to send to the server (e.g. sudo)

        Raises:
            GitlabAuthenticationError: If authentication is not correct
            GitlabTimeTrackingError: If the time tracking update cannot be done
        """
        path = '%s/%s/time_stats' % (self.manager.path, self.get_id())
        return self.manager.gitlab.http_get(path, **kwargs)

Setting a breakpoint before the return we can see that the path is ill formed:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1410507/40735199-91f8e03e-6408-11e8-8622-c6151c945ce7.png)

The problem is that the self.manager.path has a terminating `/`.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1410507/40735239-aa2610be-6408-11e8-9757-5487bfd88578.png)


## Expected Behavior


## Actual Behavior


## Specifications

  - python-gitlab version: 1.4.0
  - API version you are using (v3/v4): V4
  - Gitlab server version (or gitlab.com): GitLab.com

issue_title: Allow template type per template
issue_body:
Currently `template_type` can only appear at:

```yaml
configuration:
  template_type: ...
  ...
```

While that default is ok, it should be possible to override it per template.
e.g.
```
targets:
  - output: LICENSE
    template: license.mako
    template_type: mako
```

issue_title: BatchNorm in OP_SET version 7 has no ``mode`` attribute
issue_body:
The [BatchNormalization](https://github.com/onnx/onnx/blob/master/docs/Operators.md#batchnormalization) operator does not have ``mode`` attribute, so how do we figure out the difference between 'training' and 'testing' mode.

The [OP_SET version 6](https://github.com/onnx/onnx/blob/master/docs/Changelog.md#batchnormalization-6) of the operator had a ``is_test`` attribute.

issue_title: Review scripts using "x$var" == "string"
issue_body:
**What are you missing in citellus?**
Bash string comparison using 'x' is 'legacy' and not needed in recent shells (<10y) ( ```if [[ "x$status" == "xenabled" ]]; then ```)

**Additional info**
See here for more information: https://unix.stackexchange.com/questions/136628/bash-script-x1-x
